Japanese: ３月２２日　午後　２時１４分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: March 22, 2:14 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: まさか‥‥キリオが！ あの、華宮　霧緒が‥‥？
English: Dude! I can't believe that Adrian... No way! Not cool and collected Adrian Andrews!

Japanese: 彼女はあなたのマネージャーです。 ワナを張るのはカンタンでしょう。
English: She is your manager. It would have been very easy for her to pull this off.

Japanese: 食事で使ったナイフを手に入れる ことができるのも‥‥彼女。
English: The only person who had easy access to the knife you used at dinner was, well... Her.

Japanese: ‥‥授賞式のあとの、休憩時間‥‥
English: So after the ceremony, during the break, huh...?

Japanese: ボク、そのあいだはずっと、 ヒルネしてましたからね‥‥。
English: I was sleeping like a log the entire time...

Japanese: 血のついたボタンを、あなたの ハカマに挟むこともできた。
English: See, she could have also easily planted that blood- covered button in your hakama.

Japanese: ボクを起こしてくれたの‥‥ 彼女だったからな。
English: Hmm... Because she was the one that came to wake me up?

Japanese: じゃあ、やっぱり‥‥彼女が？
English: Then... Dude, you're saying it really was her...?

Japanese: 真犯人です。 ‥‥藤見野　イサオを殺害した。
English: Yes. She is the real killer... She was the one who murdered Juan Corrida.

Japanese: でも‥‥どうして！ アイツが藤見野を‥‥！
English: But... Why!? I thought she was buds with Juan...!

Japanese: 彼女には、動機があるんですよ。
English: She has her own agenda.

Japanese: ど、動機だって！ いったい‥‥どんな？
English: H-Her own agenda!? Wh-What are you talking about?

Japanese: ‥‥このあとの審理で アキラカになるでしょう。
English: I'm sure you'll see by the time this trial's over.

Japanese: だいじょうぶ。‥‥今日中に 無罪判決を勝ちとります。
English: It'll be alright. I'll get you acquitted by the end of today.

Japanese: 春風のようにサワヤカな 判決をおねがいしますよ！
English: Get me a verdict that's refreshing like a spring breeze, OK Mr. Lawyer dude?

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥
English: Phoenix...

Japanese: 動機‥‥天野　由利恵さんの “消えた遺書”のことよね‥‥？
English: You think her motive is related to Celeste Inpax's missing suicide note, right?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。
English: ...Yes.

Japanese: 霧緒さんは、天野　由利恵さんに 依存するようにして、生きていた。
English: Ms. Andrews depended on Ms. Inpax for her strength and will to live.

Japanese: でも、その由利恵さんが突然、 自殺してしまった。
English: But then Ms. Inpax suddenly killed herself.

Japanese: 彼女は遺書を残していたらしい。 それを、かくしてしまったのが‥‥
English: It sounds like she left a suicide note, and the person thought to have hidden it...

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオ。 ‥‥被害者というわけね‥‥。
English: ...Is Juan Corrida, the victim of this murder.

Japanese: だから、霧緒さんは 藤見野　イサオに近づいて‥‥
English: And that's why I think that Ms. Andrews got close to Mr. Corrida...

Japanese: その遺書を、なんとしてでも 取りもどそうと考えたんです！
English: All to get the suicide note back!

Japanese: そうね‥‥
English: That sounds plausible...

Japanese: ただ‥‥ひとつ、 気になることがあるわ。
English: But one thing bothers me.
Um, what...?

Japanese: そもそも、霧緒さんと由利恵さんの “依存”の関係‥‥
English: Who was it that first told us about their relationship?

Japanese: それを私たちに 教えてくれたのは‥‥だれ？
English: Better stated, Mr. Andrews' co-dependency with regards to Ms. Inpax...?

Japanese: 御剣だ‥‥
English: It was Edgeworth...

Japanese: ‥‥まだまだ、審理のゆくえは わからないわ。
English: It looks like he's still the one in command of this ship.

Japanese: 油断しないで。
English: Don't let your guard down yet.

Japanese: 同日　午後　２時２５分 地方裁判所　第３法廷
English: March 22, 2:25 PM District Court Courtroom No. 3

Japanese: では、審理を再開します。 御剣検事、おねがいします。
English: Court will now reconvene. Now then, Mr. Edgeworth, if you please.

Japanese: 法廷より要請のあった 証人を入廷させていただく。
English: The prosecution calls the witness subpoenaed by this court.

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオ氏の控え室で 事件を発見した、華宮　霧緒を！
English: Ms. Adrian Andrews, the person who discovered the crime in Mr. Juan Corrida's room!

Japanese: 証人の職業は。
English: What is your occupation?

Japanese: この事件の被告、王都楼　真悟の マネージャーをしています。
English: I am the manager of the defendant in this case, Mr. Matt Engarde.

Japanese: わかりました。では‥‥
English: I see. Now then...

Japanese: その前に。 １つ、よろしいでしょうか。
English: Before we begin, Your Honor, I have one request.

Japanese: は、はあ。 なな、なんでしょうか。
English: Uh, yes, sure. What is it?

Japanese: この場にいるみなさんが 考えていることは、わかります。
English: I'm sure everyone in this room is wondering the same thing,

Japanese: 被害者と私の関係について、 興味があるのでしょう。
English: and would love to find out more about my relationship with the victim.

Japanese: ‥‥最近は、週刊誌にも 書かれているようですから。
English: After all, it was the topic of a certain weekly "magazine" recently...

Japanese: い、いや知りませんぞ。 《ゴシップランド》なんて‥‥
English: Ah, no, I have no idea what you mean. I've never even heard of "Gossip Land"...

Japanese: （裁判長が証人になったら、 やりやすそうだな‥‥尋問）
English: (If the judge was ever a prosecution witness, he'd do all my work for me...)

Japanese: ‥‥聞かせていただこう。 被害者との関係を。
English: Anyway, I was wondering if you could please tell us about your relation to the victim.

Japanese: 私、亡くなった藤見野さんと おつきあいしていました。
English: Yes, I was seeing Mr. Corrida.

Japanese: 王都楼のライバルであることは 知っていましたけど‥‥、
English: I was also aware of the rivalry that existed between Matt and Juan...

Japanese: これは‥‥プライベートな ことですから。
English: But this was a private matter between Juan and myself.

Japanese: ふむふむ。そうでしょう。 プライでベートですからな。
English: Hmm, so it was a "fry 'n bait" matter... Or was that "bait 'n fry"? Reminds me of fishing...

Japanese: でも、私‥‥殺してません。
English: But I... But I didn't kill him.

Japanese: だれも、そんなことは言ってない。
English: No one has accused you of that.

Japanese: 言いだしそうな人がいますから。
English: I've got a feeling someone will soon...

Japanese: あなたと被害者の関係は よくわかりました。
English: I think we all understand your relationship with the victim now, Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: ‥‥では。そろそろ 証言をしていただこう。
English: Very well then. Witness, please testify to the court

Japanese: あなたが事件を発見したときの ことを、おねがいする。
English: about what happened when you discovered the murder that had taken place.

Japanese: ～事件を発見したときのこと～
English: -- When I Found the Body --

Japanese: ショーの時間になったので、控え室 の王都楼を呼びに行きました。
English: It was time for the show to start, so I went to get Matt from his room.

Japanese: そのついでに、藤見野の控え室にも 行ってみたんです。
English: After that, I went to Juan's room.

Japanese: 藤見野は、死んでいました。 私、もうショックで‥‥
English: And there was his dead body. I... I was in shock...

Japanese: そのとき私が見たのはモチロン、 現場写真とまったく同じ光景です。
English: What I saw was, naturally, the exact same scene as in the crime scene photo.

Japanese: 気絶しそうになったので、 グラスにジュースをそそぎました。
English: I felt as though I was about to faint, so I poured myself a glass of juice.

Japanese: グラスに、ジュースを‥‥？
English: You poured yourself a glass of juice...?

Japanese: はい。殺人現場にあるものに、 さわってはいけない‥‥
English: Yes. Sadly, I didn't remember not to touch things at the scene of a crime...

Japanese: そんなコトも忘れるほど ‥‥動揺していたんです。
English: And I disturbed the crime scene by moving this one thing...

Japanese: グラスの指紋は、そのとき ついたということだな。
English: And that is when the fingerprints on the wine glass were made, Your Honor.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I see.

Japanese: では、弁護人。 ‥‥尋問をおねがいします。
English: Well, Mr. Wright, you may cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: 彼女は、とても冷静で‥‥ アタマのいい女性よ。
English: She is one cool and collected customer, and she has the brains to match.

Japanese: それは‥‥わかってます。
English: Yes... I know.

Japanese: そんな証人を追いつめるには、 まともにぶつかってはダメ。
English: In order to catch a person like her, you have to avoid head-on confrontations.

Japanese: ‥‥ペースを乱すの。
English: You should disrupt her pace.

Japanese: ペースを‥‥？
English: Disrupt her pace...?

Japanese: 彼女のようなタイプは、思い通りに コトが進まないと、イラ立つはず。
English: She's the type of woman who is easily thrown off by things inconsistent with her thinking

Japanese: 彼女の予想していないところに 攻撃しなさい。
English: so you have to attack when she least expects it.

Japanese: あなたが攻撃を休んだ瞬間‥‥ 審理は終了するわ。
English: The instant you let up on your offense is the instant this trial is over. Understand?

Japanese: ～事件を発見したときのこと～
English: -- When I Found the Body --

Japanese: ショーの時間になったので、控え室 の王都楼を呼びに行きました。
English: It was time for the show to start, so I went to get Matt from his room.

Japanese: そのついでに、藤見野の控え室にも 行ってみたんです。
English: After that, I went to Juan's room.

Japanese: 藤見野は、死んでいました。 私、もうショックで‥‥
English: And there was his dead body. I... I was in shock...

Japanese: そのとき私が見たのはモチロン、 現場写真とまったく同じ光景です。
English: What I saw was, naturally, the exact same scene as in the crime scene photo.

Japanese: 気絶しそうになったので、 グラスにジュースをそそぎました。
English: I felt as though I was about to faint, so I poured myself a glass of juice.

Japanese: 花ビンを割ったのは、私です。 ギターケースの上に落として‥‥
English: I was the one who knocked the flower vase over, where it fell onto the guitar case.

Japanese: （ムジュン‥‥なんて、 見あたらないぞ‥‥）
English: (It doesn't sound like there's any glaring contradictions in her testimony just now...)

Japanese: さっきも言ったでしょ。 そうカンタンにはくずせないわ。
English: I warned you earlier that she would not crack so easily.

Japanese: カンジンなのは‥‥
English: The only way to make her is to...

Japanese: 攻撃しつづけること、ですか‥‥。
English: ...Keep on the offensive and not let up.

Japanese: 彼女を追いつめることができるのは 決定的な証拠だけ‥‥
English: The only way you're going to catch her is with some very strong, decisive evidence...

Japanese: （なんとしても、探し出す！ ‥‥真宵ちゃんのためにも‥‥）
English: (I have to find something! I just have to! ...For Maya's sake...)

Japanese: そのとき、オートロさんは‥‥？
English: And what was Mr. Engarde doing at the time...?

Japanese: 寝ていました。
English: He was taking a nap.

Japanese: 授賞式でつかれていたのでしょう。 トノサマンのまま‥‥
English: He was worn out from his mini performance as the Nickel Samurai during the ceremony...

Japanese: （オートロさんも そう言ってたな‥‥）
English: (Hmm, Mr. Engarde did say he was taking a nap...)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、彼の食器のナイフを 持ち出すことはできたわけですね？
English: Then I guess you could say it could not have been taken out of his room, yes?

Japanese: 私が‥‥ですか？ "It"...?
English: Excuse me? What are you...?

Japanese: そんな、当てこするような 質問はやめたまえ‥‥成歩堂。
English: Wright. I thought years of school would have taught you how to construct a sentence.

Japanese: それでは、私のほうから ズバリ、うかがおう。
English: If you can't make a sensible sentence with a subject, then I'll make one for you. Watch.

Japanese: あなたは、王都楼氏の ナイフを持ち出したか？
English: Did you, Ms. Andrews, remove Mr. Engarde's knife from his room?

Japanese: ‥‥いいえ。
ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 以上。証言をつづけたまえ ‥‥証人。
English: Subject, verb, object Wright. Did you skip basic grammar? The witness may continue.

Japanese: なぜ、被害者の控え室に‥‥？
English: And why did you do that...?

Japanese: 彼も、ショーに友情出演して くださるはずでしたので。
English: As a friendly gesture, Juan was to make an appearance with the other Heroes.

Japanese: （“友情”ねえ‥‥）
English: (So the show was supposed to be a "show" of friendship, huh?)

Japanese: ‥‥それだけだったのですか？
English: Is that the only reason?

Japanese: と、申しますと‥‥？
English: I beg your pardon? What are you implying...?

Japanese: あなたは、被害者に ある目的を持って近づいていた。
English: You had a certain goal in mind when you started to get close to him, correct...?

Japanese: 何か、個人的な話でも あったのでは‥‥？
English: So perhaps you had a more personal matter to discuss with the victim...?

Japanese: ザンネンですが。そのような ことはありませんでした。
English: Sorry, but I didn't have any such intentions in mind at that time.

Japanese: （‥‥決定的な証拠がなければ、 しゃべらない‥‥か）
English: (I can't get her to talk without a strong piece of evidence, I guess...)

Japanese: ‥‥では、つづけていただこう。 証人は、そこで何を見たか‥‥？
English: May we continue now...? Witness, what did you see when you got to his room?

Japanese: （やめておこう‥‥）
English: (I should back off...)

Japanese: （何か証拠がないかぎり、 この人がしゃべるとは思えない）
English: (As long as I don't have any proof, she's not going to talk.)

Japanese: ‥‥では、つづけていただこう。 証人は、そこで何を見たか‥‥？
English: May we continue now...? Witness, what did you see when you got to his room?

Japanese: “ショックを受けた”‥‥？
English: You were "in shock"...?

Japanese: いけませんか？
English: What? Was I not supposed to be?

Japanese: （霧緒さん、すごく冷静なヒト だからな。‥‥それに）
English: (Ms. Andrews is a very calculating person...)

Japanese: （イサオさんに近づいたのも、 恋愛感情があったからではない）
English: (And despite how "close" they were, I doubt she had romantic feelings for Mr. Corrida...)

Japanese: ‥‥いきなり死体を目にすれば、 どんな人間でもショックを受ける。
English: Anyone randomly stumbling upon a dead body would be in shock.

Japanese: まして、証人は若くてウツクシイ。 ‥‥ムリもないだろう。
English: And you can't seriously expect that a young beauty like her would not be shocked.

Japanese: （若さとウツクシサはこの際、 関係ないと思うけど‥‥）
English: (Somehow, I don't think beauty has anything to do with being shocked or not...)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そうですねえ‥‥。
English: Hmm... I see...

Japanese: この写真‥‥ですよね？
English: This is the photo you're referring to, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。ムネにナイフが 刺さっていて‥‥
English: Yes, the one with the knife lodged in his chest...

Japanese: この、ギターケースも？
English: And the guitar case was like this too?

Japanese: はい。もちろん、 開いていて‥‥カラッポでした。
English: Yes, it was open and empty, of course.

Japanese: ‥‥それで‥‥ 証人はどうされたか？
English: And then, what did you do next, witness?

Japanese: ‥‥ジュース‥‥？
English: Juice...?

Japanese: ええ。テーブルの上にあった トマトジュースのボトルから。
English: Yes, there was a bottle of tomato juice on the table, so I helped myself.

Japanese: でも‥‥飲まなかったんですね。
English: But you didn't drink any of it, did you?
Huh?

Japanese: このグラスに、口べにのアトが ありません。
English: There were no lip marks left on this wine glass to suggest that anyone drank from it.

Japanese: 私‥‥気が動転してました。 飲まずに置いたんです‥‥グラス。
English: I... I wasn't feeling terribly great, so I set the glass down. Without drinking it...

Japanese: どんな花ビンでしたか？
English: What kind of flower vase was it?

Japanese: ガラスの‥‥重い、 大きな花ビンでした。
English: It was a glass vase... and it was fairly big and heavy.

Japanese: 私、藤見野さんの脈を とってみようと思って‥‥
English: I thought I would try to take Juan's pulse...

Japanese: 持っていたグラスを、 ドレッサーに置いたんです。
English: So I set the glass I was holding down on the dresser.

Japanese: そのときに‥‥ヒジで 引っかけてしまって‥‥
English: And that's when my elbow accidentally hit the vase...

Japanese: （霧緒さん‥‥シッカリしてそうに 見えるんだけどな）
English: (That's odd. I thought she was always in total control of herself...?)

Japanese: それは、彼女がそう見せようと しているだけなの。
English: That's what she would like people to think.

Japanese: そのギャップに‥‥ 気をつけてね。
English: Always be mindful of the gap between the perception and reality.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。もう一度、 カクニンさせてください。
English: Ms. Andrews, I would like to confirm with you one more time.

Japanese: あなたは‥‥死体を発見して、 大きなショックを受けた。
English: When you discovered the dead body of Juan Corrida, you were in great shock.

Japanese: それで‥‥グラスにジュースを そそいだんですね？
English: And that's when you poured yourself the glass of juice, correct?

Japanese: それが何か‥‥？
English: And what of it...?

Japanese: あのときの私は、本当に アタマの中が真っ白だったんです。
English: My mind really was a complete blank at the time.

Japanese: あなたでも、そんなことが あるんですか。
English: Your mind was a complete blank?

Japanese: ‥‥とても、取り乱すようには 見えません。
English: I didn't think that was possible for you.

Japanese: シツレイですね。
English: Aren't you rude today?

Japanese: 私だって、あわてれば ミスもします。その証拠に‥‥
English: I was so dazed that I made one careless mistake... That one thing...

Japanese: “その証拠に”‥‥なんですか？
English: What "one thing"?

Japanese: あ。いえ‥‥その。 なんでもありません。
English: Um, never mind. It's no big deal.

Japanese: （何を言いかけたんだ‥‥？）
English: (What was she starting to say just now...?)

Japanese: （まあ、事件には 関係ないことだろうし‥‥）
English: (Well, it probably doesn't have anything to do with the case, so...)

Japanese: ダメよ。
English: Stop right there.

Japanese: せっかく彼女が口を すべらせてくれたんだから、
English: You finally got her to open her mouth on this one, and you're going to let it go?

Japanese: 情報は‥‥いただいて おきましょう。
English: Now is the time to get as much out of her as we can.

Japanese: わ、わかりました。
English: Y-Yeah, you're right.

Japanese: ‥‥証人！
English: Ms. Andrews!

Japanese: どうやらあなたは、現場で あわてて、何か“ミス”をした。
English: I'm convinced that as you said, you made a "mistake" at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: いったい、その“ミス”とは なんだったんですか？
English: What I really want to know is what this "mistake" was.

Japanese: それは‥‥私も 聞いていないな‥‥
English: Hmm, actually... So would I...

Japanese: す、すみません‥‥ 言いにくくて‥‥。
English: I... I'm sorry... It's just... It's kind of... embarrassing...

Japanese: 私‥‥ドレッサーに グラスを置くとき‥‥
English: When I... When I set the glass down on the dresser...

Japanese: 割ってしまったんです ‥‥その、花ビンを‥‥
English: I accidentally... knocked the flower vase over...

Japanese: か‥‥花ビンというと、 現場写真の床に写っている‥‥
English: F-Flower vase...? Are you talking about the one on the floor in the crime photo...?

Japanese: この破片‥‥ですか！
English: This mess of glass shards!?

Japanese: ドレッサーの上に 置いてあったんですけど‥‥
English: It was originally on top of the dresser...

Japanese: ヒジに引っかけてしまって‥‥ ギターケースの上に。
English: but when I bumped into it with my elbow, it fell... onto the guitar case.

Japanese: な‥‥ なぜ、そんな重要なことを！
English: Wh-Why did you withhold such an important piece of information!?

Japanese: すみません‥‥ 現場はもう、荒らされていたから、
English: I'm sorry... I thought that since the crime scene was already in disarray,

Japanese: 花ビンのひとつぐらい‥‥ いいか、と思ってしまって‥‥
English: that people would simply assume the vase was just another part of the mess.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ 新事実が発見されたようです。
English: It looks like yet another fact has come to light here.

Japanese: ‥‥証言につけくわえて いただきましょう。
English: Please add this and anything else you have to reveal to your testimony.

Japanese: すみません‥‥でも。
English: I'm sorry... But...

Japanese: その他のものは‥‥私、 さわっていませんから。
English: I have nothing more to add... I didn't touch anything else.

Japanese: 花ビンを落とした‥‥ そう証言しましたね？
English: You testified that you knocked the flower vase over. Is this correct?

Japanese: ええ。
English: Yes.

Japanese: 落としたのは、ギターケースの上 ‥‥まちがいありませんか？
English: And are you sure it fell onto the guitar case?

Japanese: な、何か問題でも‥‥？
English: I-Is there some problem with what I said...?

Japanese: “何か”どころじゃない。 重大な問題があります。
English: It's not "some" problem, it's a "major" problem.

Japanese: このケースは、フタの部分が 水でぬれていました。
English: It's true that the top of the guitar case was wet with water.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、それはおかしい！
English: However, that's exactly what is so strange!

Japanese: 霧緒さん！
English: Ms. Andrews!

Japanese: あなたは“ギターケースの上に” 花ビンを落としたと証言した！
English: You testified that the vase fell onto the guitar case!

Japanese: ‥‥それならば！
English: However!

Japanese: フタではなく、ケースの中が ぬれているはずです！
English: If that was true, the case should have gotten wet on the inside, not the outside!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに、そのとおりです！
English: Th-That's very true!

Japanese: さらに‥‥このギターケースには もう１つ、奇妙な点があります。
English: Furthermore, there is one other strange thing about this guitar case.

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんですか‥‥？
English: And wh-what is that?

Japanese: ‥‥現場写真を 見ていただきましょう。
English: Let's take another look at the crime scene photo.

Japanese: 床に、花ビンの破片が ちらばっています。
English: The remains of the vase are scattered on the floor.

Japanese: それが‥‥どうかしましたか？
English: And what is wrong with that?

Japanese: 花ビンを落としたとき、 ケースが開いていたのならば‥‥
English: If the guitar case was open when the vase fell...

Japanese: 花ビンの破片は、ギターケースの 中にあるはずです！
English: The glass shards should be INSIDE, not outside the case!

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: それがいったい、 なんだと言うのだ！
English: What is your point, Wright!?

Japanese: 証人が花ビンを落としたとき、 ギターケースは閉まっていた。
English: That the case was closed at the time the vase was knocked over?

Japanese: ‥‥それだけのことではないか！
English: Is that all!?

Japanese: しかし、霧緒さんは こう証言している！
English: No. Think back to what Ms. Andrews testified to!

Japanese: 花ビン以外のものには、 手をふれなかった、と！
English: She said that other than the vase, she didn't touch anything else!
Nngh...

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ギターケースには ふれなかった‥‥そう言いました！
English: Yes, that's right... She did implicitly say she didn't touch the guitar case!

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥ ギターケースがなんだというのだ！
English: But... But this whole matter with the guitar case is a dead end!

Japanese: まっかなギターは撮影所にあった！ 事件とはなんのカンケイもない！
English: The bright red guitar was found at the studio! It has no bearing on this case at all!

Japanese: まあ、そうですけど‥‥
English: That may very well be, however...

Japanese: （カラッポのギターケース ‥‥たしかに、関係なさそうだ）
English: (An empty guitar case does seem to have no relation to this case...)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥やはり、 たいしたイミはなさそうですね。
English: Hmm... It seems that there is no deeper meaning to the guitar case.

Japanese: どうですか？　ギターケースに ついて、詳しい証言は必要ですか？
English: Well, Mr. Wright? Do you think we need to hear more details about the guitar case?

Japanese: ケースはカラだったのですから ‥‥必要ないでしょう。
English: Well, the case was empty, and I think that's all we really need to know...

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I see.

Japanese: 待って、なるほどくん！ ‥‥忘れたの？
English: Wait! Phoenix! Don't you remember what I told you earlier?

Japanese: 大切なのは、証人の ペースをくずすこと。
English: What's important here is that you disrupt her pacing.

Japanese: 攻撃は、彼女の予想していない ところに撃ちこむの！
English: You have to attack her when she's not expecting it!

Japanese: では、次の証言に‥‥
English: Now then, let's move on to a different matter...

Japanese: ‥‥せっかくですから、 やっぱり証言をおねがいします。
English: Actually, Your Honor, I've changed my mind. I would like her to testify about the case.

Japanese: なな、な、なんですか今ごろ！
English: Wh-Wh-Why are you changing your mind NOW!?

Japanese: （裁判長のペースを くずしてしまったみたいだ‥‥）
English: (Well, looks like I disrupted the judge's pacing... Now comes the real deal... *gulp*)

Japanese: カラッポのギターケース‥‥
English: The empty guitar case...

Japanese: これは、この事件の 重要なポイントなのです！
English: I believe this is a crucial piece of the puzzle!

Japanese: ハッ！　よくそこまで ヌケヌケと言えるモノだな‥‥
English: Heh! I can't believe ANYONE would reach for straws like this! But it is you...

Japanese: （‥‥自分でもそう思う）
English: (I can't believe I'm doing this either...)

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥では、引きつづき、
English: Alright. I'll follow along... For now.

Japanese: ギターケースについて 証言をしてもらいましょう。
English: Ms. Andrews, please testify to the court about the guitar case.

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～ギターケースについて～
English: -- The Guitar Case --

Japanese: 私も、動揺していましたから。 キオクがハッキリしないんです。
English: I don't remember too clearly because I was a bit dazed.

Japanese: ケースは、私が開けたのでしょう。 ‥‥花ビンを割ったあとに。
English: I suppose I must have opened the guitar case... after I knocked the vase over.

Japanese: たいした問題ではないでしょう。 ケースはカラだったのですから。
English: It's not a big deal though, right? The case was empty after all.

Japanese: なぜ、ケースを開けたか‥‥ 自分でも、よくわかりません。
English: As for why I opened the case... Even I don't know.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥やはり、たいしたコト ではなかったようですな‥‥
English: Hmm... It looks like this really wasn't a very important point.

Japanese: とてもなつかしい気分だ。 ‥‥この、ムナしい感じ‥‥。
English: This wastefulness is such a familiar feeling by now that it's almost... comforting.

Japanese: ‥‥とりあえず尋問します。
English: Um, anyway, I'll just go ahead and start the cross- examination...

Japanese: フン。“とりあえず”か。 ‥‥私には縁のないコトバだな。
English: Hmph. Using "anyway" to change the topic; a convenient escape for a weak man.

Japanese: ～ギターケースについて～
English: -- The Guitar Case --

Japanese: 私も、動揺していましたから。 キオクがハッキリしないんです。
English: I don't remember too clearly because I was a bit dazed.

Japanese: ケースは、私が開けたのでしょう。 ‥‥花ビンを割ったあとに。
English: I suppose I must have opened the guitar case... after I knocked the vase over.

Japanese: たいした問題ではないでしょう。 ケースはカラだったのですから。
English: It's not a big deal though, right? The case was empty after all.

Japanese: なぜ、ケースを開けたか‥‥ 自分でも、よくわかりません。
English: As for why I opened the case... Even I don't know.

Japanese: さあ！　攻撃の手を 休めてはダメよ。
English: Come on, Phoenix. We can't afford to let up on her now.

Japanese: 休んでるつもりは ないんですけど‥‥
English: I wasn't planning on letting up, but...

Japanese: 相手は今、警戒していない。 ‥‥絶好のチャンスよ！
English: She's at her weakest now, so this is our chance!

Japanese: （武器があれば、だけど‥‥）
English: (Yeah, if we had a weapon to hit her with...)

Japanese: 武器なら、ちゃんとあるわ。 ‥‥法廷記録に、ね。
English: I'm sure a weapon is hiding somewhere in the Court Record, waiting to be found.

Japanese: 今はハッキリしたんですか？ キオクのほうは。
English: During your testimony just now, did you remember those events clearly in your mind?

Japanese: まだ、ちょっと‥‥
English: Well, you see...

Japanese: ギターケースは、あなたが 開けた。まちがいありませんか？
English: Are you sure you are the one who opened the guitar case?
She's...

Japanese: だれかが“答え”の指示を くれるのを、待ってるのかもね‥‥
English: She's waiting for someone to tell her if she should answer or not...

Japanese: （“依存”‥‥というヤツか‥‥）
English: (Her "co-dependency" is coming through, huh...?)

Japanese: どうなんですか？　‥‥証人。
English: Well, Ms. Andrews...?

Japanese: そ、そうですね‥‥
English: Y...Yes, it was me...

Japanese: まちがいありませんか！
English: So, you opened the guitar case then?

Japanese: え、ええ‥‥たぶん。
English: Y-Yes... Well, maybe...

Japanese: どうしてケースを開けたんですか？
English: Why did you open the guitar case?
Huh?

Japanese: 藤見野さんが亡くなって いたんですよ？
English: Mr. Corrida's dead body was right there in front of you, wasn't it?

Japanese: ギターケースを開ける前に、 人を呼ぶべきでは‥‥
English: I would think that the first thing you would do is call for help, not open a guitar case.

Japanese: ‥‥先ほどから、証人は 何度も証言している！
English: As the witness has said multiple times, when she found the dead body,

Japanese: “死体を発見して、動揺していた” ‥‥と。
English: she was "dazed".

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: たぶん、私‥‥
English: Maybe I...

Japanese: まっかなギターがブジかどうか、 気になったんだと思います。
English: Maybe I was curious to know if the bright red guitar was alright or not.

Japanese: 犯人は、それを狙っていたのかも ‥‥そう思って‥‥。
English: I thought, "Maybe the criminal took it."

Japanese: （そんな値打ちモノなのか‥‥？ まっかなギター）
English: (Why would she care about the bright red guitar...?)

Japanese: でも、とにかく‥‥
English: But, getting back on topic...

Japanese: 本当にカラだったんですか？
English: Was it really empty?

Japanese: もしかしたら‥‥
English: I was just wondering if maybe when you opened the case,

Japanese: ケースを開けたとき、 ギターはまだあったのかも‥‥
English: the guitar was still inside.

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一‥‥キサマは 何年、弁護士をやっている‥‥？
English: How long have you been a lawyer, Mr. Wright!? Have a little professionalism.

Japanese: まっかなギターは スタジオで発見された。
English: The bright red guitar was found at the studio.

Japanese: ヒトの話ぐらい、 ちゃんと聞いておけッ！
English: These trials would be over in half the time if you would just pay attention.

Japanese: 聞いておきなさい！　弁護人。
English: Yes, pay more attention, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥スミマセン‥‥
English: Sorry...

Japanese: “死体を発見して動揺していた” からですか？
English: Was that because you were "shocked and dazed" at discovering the victim's body?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。たぶん、そうです。
English: Yes... That's probably it.

Japanese: （このままつづけても、 イミがなさそうだな‥‥）
English: (I'm not going to get anywhere if I continue pressing her like this...)

Japanese: （モノを言うのは、証拠品だけ。 ‥‥ためしてみるか‥‥）
English: (The only way to make her talk is with evidence... I guess I should give it a try...)

Japanese: あなたがギターケースを 開けたはずはありません。
English: There is no way you were the one who opened the guitar case.

Japanese: ど、どうしてそんなことが‥‥！
English: Why would you say that!?

Japanese: カンタンなことです。
English: It's elementary, my dear.

Japanese: このギターケースには、被害者の 指紋しか付着していないからです！
English: Because the only fingerprints on this guitar case are those of the victim!
Ah!

Japanese: どうなのですか？　証人。
English: What is it, Ms. Andrews?

Japanese: “ケースにさわれば指紋がつく” ‥‥発想がまずしいですね。
English: You shouldn't assume that I must have left prints just because I touched the case.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 私がそのとき、手袋をしてたら モンダイないでしょう？
English: What if I were to tell you that I was wearing gloves at the time?

Japanese: てぶくろ‥‥ど、どうして そんなものを？
English: Gloves...? But why would you be wearing gloves at the time?

Japanese: あの晩は授賞式でしたから‥‥
English: It was the night of the award ceremony.

Japanese: 私もそれなりに ドレスアップしていました。
English: So of course I dressed up for the occasion.

Japanese: ‥‥たしか‥‥、うすでの 手袋をしていたと思います。
English: Yes, now I remember... I'm almost sure I was wearing a pair of thin gloves.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥なるほど‥‥
English: Hmm... I see...

Japanese: どうですか？　弁護人。証人は 現場で、手袋をしていたそうです。
English: Well, Mr. Wright. It seems the witness was wearing gloves at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: そうですね‥‥ それならば、問題ないようです。
English: Hmm, I guess so... And I guess that makes sense.

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。 中途ハンパなオトコだな。
English: Hmph. You're the epitome of a half-baked idea.

Japanese: アタマはとがらすモノではなく、 やわらかく使うものですね。
English: A brain is to be used, not to look pretty for med students carving cadavers.

Japanese: （‥‥今ひとつ、ツメが アマかったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Looks like I need to bake my thoughts just a little longer...)

Japanese: ‥‥では、証人。もう一度 証言を聞かせていただこう。
English: Now, if the defense has no further objections, let's return to the testimony.

Japanese: あなたが手袋を‥‥？ それはおかしいですね。
English: You were wearing gloves...? Isn't that a little strange?

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ！
English: Why is that strange!?

Japanese: 私が現場で、手袋を していなかった証拠でも‥‥？
English: Do you have something that would prove I was not wearing gloves at the time...?

Japanese: ‥‥今日はもう、 帰ったほうがいい、弁護人。
English: I think it's time for you to go home now, Wright...

Japanese: ‥‥その病気をうつす前に。
English: Before you hurt yourself, that is.

Japanese: （どの病気だよ‥‥！）
English: (Hurt myself...!?)

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥ムリは よくありませんよ。
English: It's alright, Mr. Wright. You don't have to push yourself so hard.

Japanese: （どうやら、ツメが今ひとつ、 アマかったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (I guess I was trying a little too hard there...)

Japanese: 証拠‥‥もちろん、ありますよ。 このグラスです。
English: I have your proof right here. This wine glass.

Japanese: ‥‥グラス‥‥
English: The wine glass?

Japanese: このグラスには、ハッキリ 残っています‥‥あなたの指紋が。
English: You left your fingerprints very clearly on this wine glass.
Ah...

Japanese: 手袋をとって、グラスに ジュースをそそぎ‥‥
English: Even if you took your gloves off when you poured yourself this glass of juice,

Japanese: ふたたび手袋をつけて、 ギターケースを開けた‥‥
English: wouldn't you think it was just a little strange...

Japanese: なぜ、そんな不自然なことを したのですか！
English: That you put your gloves back on, just to open the guitar case!?

Japanese: うぐッ！
English: Ugn!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に‥‥！
English: Order! Order! Order!!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥あなたは 正しかったようね。
English: Looks like you hit the nail on the head this time.

Japanese: ‥‥？　なんのことですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: あのギターケース‥‥ 重要な意味を持っていたのかも。
English: I believe that guitar case plays a very important role here.

Japanese: でも‥‥カラだったんですよ？
English: But it's just an empty case...

Japanese: 本当にカラだったのかしらね‥‥
English: I wonder if it really was empty though...

Japanese: だ、だって‥‥まっかなギターは スタジオにありました！
English: B-But the guitar... The bright red guitar was at the studio!

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥先入観は捨てて。
English: Phoenix, drop all of your presumptions.

Japanese: ギターケースに入るのは、 まっかなギターだけじゃないわ。
English: What was in the guitar case was not the bright red guitar.

Japanese: （まさか‥‥まっ白なギター‥‥ とか、そういう話じゃないよな）
English: (You don't mean... it was a bright WHITE guitar!? Wait, that's not right either...)

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、フシギな話です。
English: Hmm, I admit it would be unnatural for someone to do that.

Japanese: 証人は、手袋をしていなかった。 にもかかわらず、ケースには‥‥
English: So the witness was not wearing gloves, despite the fact that on the case...

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。弁護人の使う、 いつもの手だ。
English: Your Honor, this is obviously the defense's usual misdirection tactic at work.

Japanese: 関係ない方向へ目をそらし、 ウヤムヤにしてしまう‥‥
English: Steer the court towards an unrelated topic, and lull us all into his misguided...

Japanese: 証言を思い出してください！ 霧緒さんは、こう言いました。
English: No, Your Honor! Please recall that Ms. Andrews had testified that the vase

Japanese: “花ビンを、ギターケースの上に 落とした”‥‥と！
English: "fell onto the guitar case"!

Japanese: つまり、犯行があったとき、 あのケースは閉まっていたのです！
English: Which means that the case was closed when the crime took place!

Japanese: それが‥‥この写真では、 ぱっくり開いている！
English: However, it is wide open in this photo of the crime scene!

Japanese: ‥‥ギターケースは 事件に関係しているのです！
English: I am sure this guitar case has some relation to the murder!

Japanese: そこまで言うなら‥‥弁護人！
English: If you are so sure, Wright,

Japanese: キサマにはモチロン、その理由が 立証できるんだろうな！
English: then I'm sure you can somehow substantiate your outrageous claim, correct!?

Japanese: “いったいなぜ、その ギターケースは開かれたのか！”
English: Please, enlighten us as to why that guitar case has anything at all to do with this murder!
Uh...

Japanese: できるのですか！　成歩堂くん！
English: Can you do that, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: そ、そうですね‥‥
English: Um, well...

Japanese: ‥‥こういう たとえ話があります。
English: Let's suppose for a second...

Japanese: “ギターケースに入るのは、 まっかなギターだけではない”
English: That the bright red guitar was not the only thing that could have been in the case...

Japanese: “まっかなギターだけでは”‥‥？
English: The bright red guitar not being the only thing...?

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥ まっ黒なギターが入っ
English: Y-You don't mean to suggest that a bright BLACK guitar was inside the--

Japanese: このケースはカラではなかった‥‥ そう主張するつもりかッ！
English: So, you intend to push your theory that the case was not empty!? Is that it, Wright!?

Japanese: 行きがかり上、言わないワケには いかなくなったんでね！
English: I wouldn't say something I didn't intend to prove!

Japanese: イバるな！ ‥‥それなら、聞かせてもらおう。
English: Deflate that head of yours! You haven't proven anything yet!

Japanese: 事件のあったとき、このケースには いったい、何が入っていたか！
English: Now then, let's have it. What was inside this case at the time of the murder!?

Japanese: ‥‥どうしてそんなものが ギターケースに入ってるんだ？
English: And why would something like that be inside a guitar case, let alone this one?

Japanese: どうしてですか？　成歩堂くん。
English: Why, Mr. Wright, why?

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥ 可能性の話ですけどね。
English: Well... I just thought it might have been possible...?

Japanese: そんなもの、キサマのカバンに しまっておけッ！
English: I have a suggestion. Why don't you put that in the void where your brain is supposed to be!

Japanese: ‥‥そして、 二度と出さないように！
English: Yes, and never bring it out again!

Japanese: （ヒドい言われようだな‥‥）
English: (Can't a foolishly foolish fool get some love...?)

Japanese: ‥‥これでも、 まだ主張するつもりか‥‥？
English: Do you still think you can prove your theory?

Japanese: ギターケースの中は カラではなかった‥‥と？
English: Can you prove that the guitar case was not empty at the time of the murder?

Japanese: こ、これは‥‥ 写真ではないですか‥‥！
English: Th-This is... This is a photograph...!

Japanese: もちろん重要なのは、そこに 写っているシロモノです。
English: Yes, but what is important is what is in that picture, Your Honor.

Japanese: う、写っているもの‥‥ ま、まさか‥‥？
English: I-In this picture...?

Japanese: ‥‥考えるまでもありません！ あのギターケースの中には‥‥
English: It doesn't take a genius to see what I mean! What I am proposing is...

Japanese: トノサマン・丙！ ‥‥その着ぐるみが入っていた！
English: Inside the guitar case was the Nickel Samurai! The hero's very own costume!

Japanese: な、なにィッ‥‥！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: こ、これはいったい‥‥ どういうことなんですか！
English: M-Mr. Wright! Explain yourself!

Japanese: 証人はギターケースを開けて、 着ぐるみを取り出した‥‥？
English: Wright! Are you saying that the witness opened the guitar case to take out a costume!?

Japanese: いったい、なんのために そんなことを！
English: What insane point would there be to doing something like that!?

Japanese: ‥‥それはもちろん、 着るためでしょう。
English: That insane point would be to wear the costume, of course.

Japanese: その中にかくれて、 現場から逃げるためですよ。
English: Ms. Andrews put it on to hide her identity so she could make her escape.

Japanese: カオを見られるワケには いかなかったでしょうからね。
English: After all, you couldn't let anyone see you leave, could you, Ms. Andrews?

Japanese: ば‥‥バカな！
English: I-I refuse to accept your theory!

Japanese: そんな‥‥そんな証拠は あるのかッ！
English: Do you have anything to support such a preposterous idea!?

Japanese: 控え室のそとでは、スクープを ねらうカメラマンがいた。
English: Just outside the door was an investigative photographer who was starving for a big scoop.

Japanese: そして実際に、写真を 撮られているじゃないですか。
English: And in the end, she managed to get this shot, correct?

Japanese: それが‥‥それが、 この写真だと言うのですか！
English: You... You mean this photo!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛にィィィッ！
English: Order! Ordeeeer!

Japanese: ま、また、ややこしいコトに なってきましたね‥‥。
English: It looks like we've wandered into quite another mess again, haven't we...?

Japanese: さすがね‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Nice job, Phoenix.

Japanese: アタマで考えるより先に、 口が動いていました！
English: Well, you know my strategy. Speak first, think later!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥真犯人は、 着ぐるみの中に入って‥‥
English: Hmm... So the real murderer was hiding inside a costume...

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待て！
English: W-Wait one second, Your Honor!

Japanese: トノサマンの着ぐるみは、 王都楼　真悟のものだ！
English: The Nickel Samurai's costume would have been Mr. Matt Engarde's!

Japanese: なぜそんなものが、 被害者の控え室にあるのだ！
English: Why would something of the defendant's be in the victim's room!?

Japanese: しかも、ギターケースの中に かくされていた、などと！
English: And inside the guitar case, of all places!?

Japanese: そうですね‥‥。 たしかに、ちょっとフシギです。
English: Hmm, true... That is a little baffling...

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥あなたの考えは いかがですか？
English: Mr. Wright, the court would like to hear your thoughts.

Japanese: ギターケースの中のトノサマン ‥‥この着ぐるみは、いったい？
English: What was this Nickel Samurai costume doing inside the guitar case...?

Japanese: おそらく、オートロさんの 着ぐるみを盗んだのでは‥‥
English: I think it may have been stolen from Mr. Engarde...

Japanese: ぬぬ、盗んだ！ なぜ、そんなコトを！
English: S-Stolen!? But why would someone do such a thing!?

Japanese: 受賞記念のショーをジャマするため ‥‥ではないでしょうか。
English: Maybe the thief wanted to interfere with the post- ceremony stage show.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 着ぐるみがなければ、オートロさん はハダカで出ていくしかない！
English: And so, if he didn't have his costume, then Mr. Engarde would be forced to go naked!

Japanese: 弁護人！　キサマがこの場から ハダカで出て行けッ！
English: Maybe YOU should be stripped naked and run out for making a mockery of this court!

Japanese: なな、なんだと！
English: E-EXCUSE ME!?

Japanese: 授賞式のあと、王都楼　真悟は 着ぐるみを脱がなかった！
English: Mr. Matt Engarde did not take his costume off after the award ceremony!

Japanese: ‥‥そのままのカッコウで ヒルネをしていた‥‥
English: He even testified that he took a nap while still wearing his costume!

Japanese: そう証言していたではないかッ！
English: Is this not correct!?

Japanese: そう証言してましたっけね。
English: Ah, yeah, he did say something like that, didn't he?

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥ そんなコトだろうと思いました。
English: *sigh* That's what I thought.

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、やり直しましょう。
English: Let's try that one more time, shall we, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 休憩中、オートロさんは 着ぐるみを脱がなかった‥‥
English: Mr. Engarde did not take his costume off during the break period...

Japanese: それならば当然‥‥別の着ぐるみ だったことになります。
English: In that case, the costume we are talking about was a spare one.

Japanese: なんだと‥‥
English: What...?

Japanese: では‥‥事件当夜、 ホテル・バンドーには‥‥
English: Then... are you saying that on the night of the murder...

Japanese: トノサマンの着ぐるみが ２体あった、と言うのですか！
English: there were TWO Nickel Samurai costumes at the Gatewater Hotel!?

Japanese: そういうことになります。
English: Yes, that is what I am saying.

Japanese: しかも、ギターケースに入っていた ‥‥ということは‥‥
English: And how do you explain the costume that was inside the guitar case...?

Japanese: 被害者の藤見野　イサオが、 計画的に持ちこんだことになる！
English: It would mean that the victim himself had planned to bring this spare to the ceremony!

Japanese: しかし‥‥いったい、なぜ！
English: But... But why!?

Japanese: 被害者の藤見野　イサオさんは 《忍者ナンジャ》の役者さんです。
English: The victim, Mr. Corrida, was the Jammin' Ninja.

Japanese: トノサマンの着ぐるみを コッソリ持ちこんだ‥‥
English: Why would he secretly bring the Nickel Samurai's spare costume with him...?

Japanese: その理由は、いったいなんですか？
English: What could be the reason behind such a peculiar act?

Japanese: そこなんですよねえ。
English: Ah, so that's what he intended...

Japanese: なな、なぜそこで考えこむ！
English: Wh-What are you mumbling to yourself about now!?

Japanese: キサマ‥‥今まで、テキトーに 考えながらしゃべっていたのか！
English: Have you just been rambling all this time without any sense of inner monologue!?

Japanese: え！　そ、そんなことは‥‥
English: Huh!? N-No, I just...

Japanese: 弁護人。教えてください！
English: Mr. Wright, please explain yourself!

Japanese: 被害者はなぜ、トノサマンの 着ぐるみを持ちこんだのですか！
English: Why do you think the victim had the Nickel Samurai's spare costume!?

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥だいじょうぶ！ あなたなら、わかるはずよ。
English: Phoenix... Are you sure you can explain this one?

Japanese: よく考えて。 ‥‥そして、勇気を持つの！
English: Think carefully before you answer... And then answer with gusto! I believe in you!

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥ぼくの考えを言いましょう。
English: Alright. This is what I think.

Japanese: 被害者がトノサマンの着ぐるみを 会場に持ちこんだ、その理由は‥‥
English: The reason the victim brought the Nickel Samurai's spare costume to the hotel was...

Japanese: なるほどくん！　何してるのッ！
English: Phoenix! What are you doing!? Are you crazy!?

Japanese: は、はいッ！
ぼくなりに勇気を持って、 投げつけてみました！
English: I presented the evidence with gusto, just like you said!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに“勇気を持て” とは言ったけど‥‥
English: I told you to present it with gusto, yes...

Japanese: その前に、“よく考えて”って 言ったでしょ！
English: But I seem to recall the sentence before that was, "Think carefully"!

Japanese: え。
English: *gulp*

Japanese: なんですかこれは！　弁護人！
English: Wh-What is the meaning of this, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: あ。す、すいません‥‥。
English: Ah, sorry...

Japanese: ここは、かなりムズカシイわ。 法廷記録をよく見て。
English: This one is pretty tough. I really think you should look carefully at the Court Record.

Japanese: そして‥‥思い出すの！
English: And then search through your memories! The answer is there, I know you'll find it!

Japanese: ‥‥では、 よろしいですか？　弁護人！
English: Now then. Are you ready to try again, Mr. Wright? Minus the wrong evidence this time?

Japanese: これは‥‥なんですか？
English: What is this...?

Japanese: 事件当夜、 アトラクション・ショーのあと‥‥
English: On the night of the murder, after the stage show,

Japanese: トノサマンによる、記者会見が おこなわれるはずでした。
English: the Nickel Samurai was going to hold a special press conference.

Japanese: 記者会見‥‥？
English: A press conference...?

Japanese: トノサマンが、何かを “告白する”という内容です。
English: Yes, the Nickel Samurai was supposed to confess something at this conference.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、 そんな報告を受けている。
English: I heard about this as well. For once, you're not making something up, Wright.

Japanese: しかし、フシギなことに‥‥
English: But what struck me as strange was that Mr. Engarde himself

Japanese: オートロさん自身は、記者会見の ことを知らない、と言っています。
English: said he had no idea he was supposed to be holding a press conference that night.

Japanese: それはいったい ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: But how can that be!?

Japanese: コタエは、１つしかありません。 会見をセッティングしたのは‥‥
English: The way I see it, that can mean only one thing: The conference was set up

Japanese: 被害者の、藤見野　イサオさん だったのです！
English: by none other than the victim, Mr. Juan Corrida himself!

Japanese: ‥‥ひ、被害者が？
English: Th-The victim?

Japanese: そのとおり！
English: Yes.

Japanese: ２体目のトノサマンの着ぐるみは、 そのために用意された！
English: The spare Nickel Samurai costume was prepared for that very conference!

Japanese: イサオさんは、トノサマンとして 記者会見をしようとしたのです！
English: Mr. Corrida was going to hold the press conference as the "Nickel Samurai"!

Japanese: トノサマンになりすまして、 記者会見を‥‥！
English: He was going to dress up as the Nickel Samurai and hold a conference!?

Japanese: しかし、どうして被害者は そんなことを考えたのですか！
English: But why would the victim do such a thing!?

Japanese: それはわかりません。 ただ‥‥
English: That's something I don't quite know yet, however...

Japanese: ‥‥そこで気になるのが、 記者会見の内容です。
English: What I am concerned with right now is what he intended to reveal at that conference.

Japanese: “トノサマンが 何かを告白する”‥‥
English: The Nickel Samurai was going to "confess" something.

Japanese: “告白”とは、自分のことを しゃべるものだ。
English: And by "confess", I'd wager he was going to reveal something about "himself".

Japanese: つまり藤見野　イサオは、 トノサマンの着ぐるみを着て‥‥、
English: Which means that Juan Corrida, posing as the Nickel Samurai,

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟のことを 語ろうとしていた！
English: was going to speak about Matt Engarde!

Japanese: ‥‥そうなりますね‥‥
English: Yes, I guess that is what it would mean...

Japanese: もし、そうだったのなら‥‥
English: But, if that's the case...

Japanese: それは“告白”とは言わない。 ‥‥“告発”ではないかッ！
English: that's not a "confession"! That's "public disclosure"!

Japanese: ‥‥ふう。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: しょ‥‥証人‥‥？
English: M-Ms. Andrews...?

Japanese: さすがみなさん、プロですね‥‥
English: I can see why you are pros at what you do.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか？
English: Pardon me?

Japanese: おっしゃるとおり‥‥記者会見は、 藤見野がセットしたものです。
English: Yes, just as you say, the press conference was set up by Juan.

Japanese: 証人！　説明をおねがいします！
English: Ms. Andrews! Please offer us an explanation for this!

Japanese: 藤見野にたのまれて、記者会見の 手配をしたのは、私です。
English: I was the one he asked to help set it up.

Japanese: ２体目のトノサマンを 用意したのも‥‥私。
English: And the person who prepared the second costume for him... That was also me.

Japanese: あなたが‥‥！
藤見野は、《忍者ナンジャ》に すべてを賭けていました。
English: Juan had bet everything on the Jammin' Ninja this year.

Japanese: ‥‥もし、今年のグランプリを 逃したら、そのときは‥‥
English: And if he lost the Grand Prix...

Japanese: 王都楼自身を失脚させてやる。 ‥‥そう考えていたようです。
English: He was going to make sure Matt was going down with him. That's what he thought anyway.

Japanese: しっきゃく‥‥ですか‥‥
English: He was going to ruin him, huh...?

Japanese: どうやら藤見野は‥‥
English: It looked like somehow, Juan had in his hands a secret so powerful...

Japanese: 王都楼の芸能生命を断つような ヒミツを‥‥にぎっていたのです！
English: that it would destroy Matt's acting career had it been revealed!

Japanese: ‥‥なんですって！
English: What!?

Japanese: 証人！　王都楼さんの“ヒミツ” ‥‥それは、いったい？
English: And do you know what this "secret" of Mr. Engarde's is, Ms. Andrews...?

Japanese: それは、藤見野だけのヒミツです。 ‥‥私は知りません。
English: That's something only Juan knew. I... I don't know what it is.

Japanese: ああ‥‥そうですか。
English: Ah... I see.

Japanese: 私‥‥みなさんに、疑われるような タイドだったかもしれませんね。
English: I... I've probably been coming off quite suspicious to everyone,

Japanese: でも‥‥しかたなかったんです。
English: but that's to be expected.

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟をかばうため、 でしたから‥‥。
English: I've been trying to protect Matt, after all...

Japanese: オートロさんを‥‥かばう！
English: P-Protect Mr. Engarde!?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、私たちの知らない ジジツがあったようですね‥‥
English: And yet again another strange bit of truth comes to light it seems...

Japanese: 証人！　あなたの行動について、 本当のところを証言してください！
English: Ms. Andrews, if you could, please tell us the truth about your behavior!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました‥‥
English: Yes, Your Honor. I understand.

Japanese: ～王都楼をかばったこと～
English: -- Protecting Matt --

Japanese: 現場を見た瞬間から、王都楼が 犯人だと感じました。
English: From the moment I saw the crime scene, I had a feeling that Matt was the murderer.

Japanese: 王都楼は、なんとしても藤見野を 殺害しなければならなかった。
English: Matt had to kill Juan no matter what.

Japanese: それに、彼には犯行時刻の アリバイもありません。
English: And he didn't have an alibi for what he was doing at the time of the murder.

Japanese: 決定的と言えば、やはり証拠品。 あの、ボタンやナイフ‥‥
English: My thoughts were confirmed by the evidence, of course; the button and the knife...

Japanese: 私、王都楼のマネージャーだから ‥‥彼を守らなければ、と思って。
English: But I'm Matt's manager... So I felt that I had to protect him...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥たしかに、 すべてそろっていますな‥‥
English: Hmm... This does account for everything...

Japanese: 私‥‥ロジックでモノを 考えるタイプですから。
English: Well... I am the logical type.

Japanese: ついに本性をあらわしたわね ‥‥彼女。
English: We're finally seeing her true self.

Japanese: キケンな状況よ。とても。
English: She is more nervous than a scared rabbit.

Japanese: この証言に問題がなければ‥‥ 判決を下すことも可能でしょう。
English: If there are no objections, I feel that I can pass a verdict based on this testimony.

Japanese: では、弁護人。尋問を。
English: Now then, Mr. Wright, if you please.

Japanese: （いつの間にか、状況は 逆転しているみたいだ‥‥）
English: (Looks like somehow everything has swung to the opposite end of the scale again...)

Japanese: （彼女の“ロジック”‥‥ かならず、ひっくり返す！）
English: (That just means I have to put my weight into this and turn her logic upside-down!)

Japanese: ～王都楼をかばったこと～
English: -- Protecting Matt --

Japanese: 現場を見た瞬間から、王都楼が 犯人だと感じました。
English: From the moment I saw the crime scene, I had a feeling that Matt was the murderer.

Japanese: それに、彼には犯行時刻の アリバイもありません。
English: And he didn't have an alibi for what he was doing at the time of the murder.

Japanese: 私、王都楼のマネージャーだから ‥‥彼を守らなければ、と思って。
English: But I'm Matt's manager... So I felt that I had to protect him...

Japanese: さすがに‥‥ もっともらしく聞こえますね。
English: As I expected, Ms. Andrews' testimony seems pretty solid.

Japanese: そうかしら？　私には、 アイマイな証言に聞こえるけど。
English: Really? Because to me, it sounded a little wishy- washy.

Japanese: アイマイ‥‥ （少し、ゆさぶってみるか‥‥）
English: Wishy-washy? (Well, I guess we'll see if I press a little more...)

Japanese: ‥‥もう、わかってるわね。
English: You should know this by now,

Japanese: 彼女をだまらせるには、 ハッキリした証拠が必要よ。
English: but you'll need strong, decisive evidence to make her talk.

Japanese: はい！ （‥‥ねじ伏せてやるさ！）
English: Got it, Chief! (...I'm going to pin you down this time, Ms. Andrews!)

Japanese: 王都楼は、なんとしても藤見野を 殺害しなければならなかった。
English: Matt had to kill Juan no matter what.

Japanese: その動機も知っています。 ‥‥確証はありませんけど。
English: I know what his motive was. ...But I don't have any way to prove I'm right.

Japanese: 決定的と言えば、やはり証拠品。 あの、ボタンやナイフ‥‥
English: My thoughts were confirmed by the evidence, of course; the button and the knife...

Japanese: あのボタンは、王都楼が被害者と 格闘中にむしり取ったものです。
English: That button was torn off of Juan during his fight with Matt.

Japanese: それは‥‥カン、という ヤツですか？
English: Would you say that was your "intuition" speaking to you?

Japanese: あなたといっしょに しないでください。
English: Don't confuse my methods of reasoning with your own.

Japanese: うぐ。
English: Urk...

Japanese: 犯行を立証するには、 ３つの要素が必要です。
English: If you want to prove that someone did something, you need three things.

Japanese: みっつ‥‥？
English: Three... things?

Japanese: “動機”“犯行のチャンス” そして‥‥“物的証拠”。
English: A motive, an opportunity to commit the crime, and finally... decisive evidence.

Japanese: それらを考えれば、 コタエはアキラカです。
English: And if you think these three things through, the answer becomes quite clear.

Japanese: 知ってたかしら？ なるほどくん。
English: You should have already known that, Phoenix...

Japanese: ‥‥学校では 教えてくれませんでした。
English: They didn't teach that to us in school... At least, not from what I remember...

Japanese: つづけてよろしいですか？
English: May I continue now?

Japanese: あなたの言う“殺害する理由” ‥‥やはり、記者会見ですか？
English: So would you say this "need" came from the press conference?

Japanese: そう。なぜ藤見野が、あの場所で 会見をセッティングしたのか？
English: Yes. Do you know why Juan chose that event and that hotel for the conference?

Japanese: 得意の絶頂にいる王都楼に、 決定的なダメージを与えるためよ。
English: Because that was when he could cause the most damage to the public's beloved Matt Engarde.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、その計画を 知っていたのですね？
English: And you knew of this plan, didn't you, Ms. Andrews?

Japanese: はい‥‥。会見や着ぐるみを 手配したのは、私ですから。
English: Yes... because I was the one who set up the conference and prepared the costume.

Japanese: でも‥‥その会見のこと、オートロ さんは知らなかったはずです。
English: But... I'm sure Mr. Engarde himself didn't know anything about a press conference.

Japanese: ‥‥さあ、それはどうでしょうか？
English: Oh really?

Japanese: “知らなかった”という証拠でも 見せていただけるのかしら？
English: Can you show me any proof that he didn't know about the press conference?

Japanese: と、とにかく！　確証がない以上、 証言として認められません！
English: A-Anyway! The important thing here is that this information was not in your testimony!

Japanese: わかりました。‥‥証人、 証言を修正してください。
English: Yes, I agree. Ms. Andrews, please correct your testimony, if you please.

Japanese: “苦しまぎれ”って、 こういうことを言うんですね‥‥
English: "Grasping at straws" now, are we, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: で、でも‥‥あなたは 証言したじゃないですか！
English: B-But didn't you already testify earlier that Mr. Engarde was...

Japanese: オートロさんは、控え室で ヒルネをしていた、と！
English: "Taking a nap in his room"!?

Japanese: あれも、彼をかばうために ついたウソだと言うのですか！
English: Are you telling me now that, that too, was a lie so you could cover for Mr. Engarde!?

Japanese: そんなことはありません。
English: I'm not telling you anything of the sort.

Japanese: 私が呼びに行ったとき、彼は たしかに、寝ていました。
English: When I went to get him for the show, he honestly was sleeping.

Japanese: でも、ずっと寝ていたかどうか ‥‥それは、知りません。
English: However, as to whether he was sleeping the entire time, that I cannot say.

Japanese: 私は、ステージのセッティングを していましたから。
English: I was too busy setting up the stage at the time.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥。聞けば聞くほど、 スキがありませんねえ。
English: Hmm... I keep trying, but I can find no flaws with what Ms. Andrews has said.

Japanese: だれかさんとは、大ちがいです。
English: I can't say the same for some people here in this courtroom, however.

Japanese: （裁判長が‥‥まっすぐ 千尋さんをニラみつけている）
English: (The judge is glaring straight at Mia...)

Japanese: あなたでしょ！
English: He's glaring at you, smart guy!

Japanese: 少なくともナイフは、 決定的とは言えないはずです！
English: You can hardly call the knife "decisive evidence"!

Japanese: ナイフについていた指紋は、 偽装の可能性がある！
English: The fingerprints on the knife could very well be a clever camouflage!

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥ボタンのほうは どうなのでしょうか？
English: Then... What about the button?

Japanese: ボタン‥‥
English: The button...?

Japanese: 現場の状況から、被害者と犯人が 争ったのは明白です。
English: It's clear from the crime scene that the victim and the murderer fought.

Japanese: その最中に犯人は、忍者ナンジャの 衣装からボタンをむしり取った。
English: And during the fight, the killer ripped the button from the Jammin' Ninja's costume.

Japanese: コレのことですな。
English: You're talking about this button, correct?

Japanese: そのボタン、王都楼のハカマから 発見されたのでしょう？
English: That button was found in the pleats of Matt's hakama, isn't that correct?

Japanese: 決定的な証拠だと思いますけど。
English: I would think that makes it very decisive evidence.

Japanese: うっ。
English: Urk.

Japanese: またまた、やりこめられましたね、 ‥‥弁護人。
English: Looks like you were out-foxed again, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: とと、とにかく！　ナイフは 証拠になりません！
English: A-Anyway! The knife doesn't prove a thing!

Japanese: 証言を修正してください！
English: Please fix your testimony!

Japanese: スナオに負けを認められない 男性‥‥見てられませんね。
English: I can't stand the sight of a man who can't gracefully accept his defeat.

Japanese: （‥‥よかった。千尋さんは ぼくを見てくれてるぞ）
English: Thank goodness Mia can still look at me...)

Japanese: 冷ややかな目で。
English: With an icy stare, yes.

Japanese: では、証人。 証言を修正してください。
English: Ms. Andrews, for Mr. Wright's sake, please add this information to your testimony.

Japanese: でも、ケッキョクこうして、 彼をウラ切るわけですね？
English: But what you really did was stab the guy in the back, didn't you?

Japanese: ‥‥サイアクのタイミングで。
English: And at the worst possible time.

Japanese: “ウラ切る”という言いかたは いかがなものだろうか。
English: Who's to say she really "stabbed the guy in the back" as you put it?

Japanese: ‥‥この証人がオートロさんを 告発するなら、いつでもできた。
English: This witness could have disclosed things about Mr. Engarde at any time.

Japanese: なぜ、今‥‥たくさんのヒトが 見ている前でぶちまけたのか？
English: Why, then, would she wait until there was a large audience before doing so?

Japanese: ‥‥藤見野さんが計画していた 記者会見と同じです。
English: It's the same reason why Mr. Corrida planned such an elaborate conference.

Japanese: 彼女は、オートロさんに決定的な ダメージを与えようとした！
English: Ms. Andrews wanted to cause Mr. Engarde as much damage as she possibly could!

Japanese: この証人は、 被告人に敵意を持っている！
English: This witness bears ill-will towards the defendant!

Japanese: この場は“成歩堂の推理を聞く会” ではない。
English: This isn't the "Phoenix Wright Wax Philosophical Power Hour".

Japanese: ‥‥証人を中傷するのは やめてもらおうか。
English: And please, stop slandering the witness.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、オートロさんに ウラミでもあるんですか？
English: Has Mr. Engarde done something to hurt or betray you personally?

Japanese: どうしてでしょうか。
English: Why do you ask?

Japanese: 藤見野さんの記者会見‥‥、 あなたが手配したんですよね。
English: You were the one who helped Mr. Corrida with his press conference...

Japanese: それはつまり、オートロさんを失脚 させる、手伝いをしたことになる！
English: And that event was supposed to bring down Mr. Engarde, yet you still helped out!

Japanese: 今、裁かれているのは、 王都楼　真悟だ‥‥
English: The person on trial right now is Mr. Engarde, Wright.

Japanese: この証人が何を考えて 被害者に手を貸したのか‥‥
English: What the witness was thinking, helping the victim with his plan,

Japanese: それは、問題ではない。
English: is none of our concern.

Japanese: とにかく、王都楼　真悟には 動機があったのだ。
English: In any case, this means that the defendant had a motive to kill.

Japanese: （‥‥まずいな‥‥）
English: (Why do I keep doing this to myself...?)

Japanese: どうしてそんなことが わかるんですか？
English: And how do you know that?

Japanese: ボタンの糸と、むしり取られた 糸の先を照合した結果‥‥
English: When the ends of the threads on the button and the costume were matched up,

Japanese: 一致した‥‥と聞きました。
English: they were found to fit together perfectly... Or so I heard.

Japanese: （それはぼくも聞いている）
English: (Hmm, I've heard that before too.)

Japanese: （でも‥‥なんで霧緒さんは、 こんなに事件に詳しいんだ？）
English: (But why would Ms. Andrews know about this case down to such a fine detail?)

Japanese: 中途ハンパがキライですから ‥‥私。
English: Don't look at me like that! Just because I'm prepared and you are not!

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥ つけいるスキがないぞ）
English: (Argh... And I thought I had her this time for sure.)

Japanese: （何かあるとすれば、ここしか ないと思うんだけど‥‥）
English: (If there's anything to trip her up on, it has to be here. ...But where and what?)

Japanese: ‥‥これは、被害者の 解剖記録です。
English: This is the victim's autopsy report.

Japanese: ここに、ハッキリ書かれています。 死因は“マフラーで絞殺”と。
English: It clearly states that the cause of death was "strangulation by a scarf".
S...Strangulation...

Japanese: 被害者にナイフが刺されたのは、 すでに死んだ後だったのです。
English: The knife stab to the victim was done after the victim had already died.

Japanese: そ、それがどうかしましたか？
English: A-And what does that mean?

Japanese: さて‥‥。このボタンには、 被害者の血がついてます。
English: Let's examine the evidence. This button has the victim's blood on it.

Japanese: ということは、コイツが衣装から むしり取られたのは‥‥？
English: Which would mean that it was ripped off of the costume... when?

Japanese: ‥‥ナイフで刺された後、 ということになる‥‥。
English: After the knife was stabbed into the victim...

Japanese: そのとおり！　‥‥つまり‥‥
English: Exactly! Which means...

Japanese: このボタンが、格闘中に むしり取られたはずはない！
English: It is impossible that this button was torn off during the victim's final struggle,

Japanese: そのとき被害者は、すでに 絞殺されていたんですからね！
English: because the victim was strangled to death in that fight!
Ah...

Japanese: ‥‥そうなんですよ、霧緒さん。
English: That's right, Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: 格闘中に、このボタンが むしり取れるはずがないんです。
English: There is no way this button was ripped off during the struggle.

Japanese: ボタンは‥‥死体から ムリヤリ、引きちぎられたんです！
English: This button was consciously pulled off of the victim's already dead body!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥ど、どういうこと
English: Order! Order! Wh-What is the meaning--

Japanese: どういうことだ！　弁護人！
English: What is the meaning of this, Wright!?

Japanese: そのボタンが、被害者の死後 むしり取られたからといって‥‥
English: So what if the button was torn off the body after the victim had already died?

Japanese: 何が変わるというのだッ！
English: What does that change!?

Japanese: では、１つ尋ねようか ‥‥御剣検事。
English: Let me ask you one simple question, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ボタンは、なんのために ひきちぎられたんだろう？
English: Why was the button torn off? What purpose did that serve?

Japanese: な、“なんのため”‥‥だと？
English: What "purpose"...?

Japanese: ボタンは、格闘中に ひきちぎられたのではない。
English: We now know this button was not torn off during the fight.

Japanese: 犯人はわざわざ、コイツを 死体からむしり取ったわけだ。
English: So the murderer took the time and effort to purposely rip this from the victim's body.

Japanese: そこには当然‥‥ 目的があったはずだろう？
English: That would mean that the murderer had something in mind, wouldn't it?

Japanese: 弁護人！　あなたには‥‥
English: Mr. Wright! Does this mean...

Japanese: あなたには、わかるのですか！ 犯人の‥‥“目的”が！
English: Does this mean you know what the murderer wanted to do with this button!? What was it!?

Japanese: おそらく犯人は、はじめての 犯行で、まい上がっていた。
English: Maybe this was the criminal's first crime and since it went so well,

Japanese: そこで、何か記念品が ほしくなったのではないかと‥‥
English: the person wanted something as a memento...

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士。
English: Wright...

Japanese: このボタン、記念として キサマにくれてやる。
English: I'll give this button to you as a memento.

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: キサマが法廷を永久に去る 記念品だッ！
English: A reminder to never set foot in another court of law again for as long as you live!

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。 あなたは、もう来なくていいです。
English: Yes... I think it would be for the best if you stayed away from the attorney's bench.

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ってください！ ‥‥考えなおします。
English: W-W-Wait! Please, Your Honor! I... I'll get it right this time! I'm sure of it!

Japanese: もちろん、証拠のいん滅です！
English: It was to destroy the evidence, of course!

Japanese: あの‥‥証拠のいん滅‥‥
English: Um... To destroy the evidence...

Japanese: それは聞こえている！
English: Yes, we heard you the first time!

Japanese: いったい、なんの証拠を いん滅したと言うのだ！
English: And which blasted piece of evidence was the person trying to destroy, Wright!?

Japanese: ‥‥ううう‥‥
English: Umm...

Japanese: 弁護人！　そのうち、 あなた自身がいん滅されますぞ！
English: Mr. Wright! If you continue like this, I'm afraid you'll only destroy yourself!

Japanese: す、すみません‥‥
English: S-Sorry...

Japanese: 考えられる目的は、ただ１つ！
English: There is only one logical reason for doing something like that.

Japanese: もちろん‥‥王都楼さんに 罪を着せるためです。
English: It was to pin the crime on Mr. Engarde!

Japanese: むしったボタンを、自分の ハカマに挟む犯人はいません！
English: There is no way anyone would put a bloodied button in their own pants!

Japanese: そう！　王都楼さんは ワナにはめられたのです！
English: That's right! Mr. Engarde was set up!

Japanese: ‥‥真犯人によって！
English: By the real killer, of course!

Japanese: その‥‥真犯人とは‥‥
English: And... The real murder is...?

Japanese: いったい、だれなんですかッ！
English: Well, Mr. Wright!? Who in the world is the real killer then!?

Japanese: （ついに‥‥）
English: (Finally...)

Japanese: （ここまで こぎつけることができたか‥‥）
English: (I can't believe I managed to bring this trial all the way up to this point...)

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥ 最後まで、気を抜かないでね！
English: Phoenix... You can't let your guard down yet! Not until the very end!

Japanese: オートロさんに罪を着せようと たくらんだ、真犯人は‥‥！
English: The real killer, the person who planned to frame Mr. Engarde is...

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士。
English: Wright.

Japanese: 次の３つのうち、仲間はずれは？ りんご　　みかん　　成歩堂
English: Which of these four doesn't belong here? Up, down, left, Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥そりゃ、“成歩堂”だろ？
English: Um, "Wright"?

Japanese: ‥‥安心した。さすがに、 それぐらいはわかるようだな。
English: Thank you, I feel much better. I'm relieved to know you can at least pick that much out.

Japanese: ‥‥キサマは、あらゆるイミで 仲間はずれなのだ！
English: I worry about you. You seem to fail every time you try to make logical sense!

Japanese: 早い話、“ちゃんと考えろ！” ということよ。
English: Or in other words, "think before you speak", Phoenix.

Japanese: （だんだん、 手がこんできたな‥‥）
English: (Is it just me or is everyone ganging up on me?)

Japanese: 弁護人。早くしてください。
English: That's enough dawdling now, Mr. Wright. We don't have all day.

Japanese: は‥‥はい。
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥華宮　霧緒さん！
English: Ms. Adrian Andrews!

Japanese: あなたを、藤見野　イサオ殺害の 犯人として、告発します！
English: I choose you! You are Mr. Corrida's killer!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥成歩堂くん！
English: Order! Order! Order!! Mr. Wright!

Japanese: これは‥‥重大なコトです！ 何か、証拠でも‥‥
English: This is a very grave matter! Do you have any evidence that supports your charge...?
"Any evidence"...?

Japanese: すべての証拠が、彼女‥‥ 華宮　霧緒を示している！
English: ALL of the evidence points to Ms. Andrews!

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥そんな ハッタリ、私には通用しないわ！
English: Wh... How preposterous! You can't stick any of that on me!

Japanese: ハッタリかどうか‥‥ ためしてみましょうか？
English: I can't, can I? Would you care to test me?

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥このナイフは！
English: Then... Then what about this knife!?

Japanese: ‥‥ナイフは、被害者がマフラーで 絞殺されたあと、刺されました。
English: The knife was used to stab the victim after he had already been strangled to death.

Japanese: もちろん、オートロさんに疑いが かかるようにするためです。
English: It was used to throw suspicion onto Mr. Engarde, naturally.

Japanese: 彼の指紋がついたナイフは、 控え室にあった。
English: A knife covered in the defendant's fingerprints could only be taken from his room.

Japanese: それを知っていたのは‥‥ いっしょに食事をした、あなただ。
English: And the only one who had dinner with him, and knew which knife to take, was you.
...Tsk!

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ！
English: Th-Then!

Japanese: この‥‥ハカマから見つかった ボタンはどうなんですか！
English: What... What about the button that was found in Matt's hakama!?

Japanese: そのボタンは、被害者の死後、 むしりとられた。
English: This button was removed from the victim's body after he had already died.

Japanese: そんなことができるのは、 犯人か発見者しかいません。
English: The only people who could've done so were the person who found his body or the killer.

Japanese: しかし‥‥、
English: However, if Mr. Engarde was the real killer,

Japanese: もしオートロさんが犯人なら、 自分のハカマに挟むハズがない。
English: there is no way he would have put such incriminating evidence in his own hakama!
Ughn...

Japanese: あのボタンを、オートロさんの ハカマに挟むことができたのは‥‥
English: The only person who could have put this button into Mr. Engarde's hakama,

Japanese: ヒルネしている彼を起こした人間。 ‥‥やはり、あなたです。
English: is the person who went to wake him from his nap... which is you, yet again, Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: な、なるほど‥‥ では、カラのギターケースは‥‥
English: I... I see... What about the empty guitar case...?

Japanese: それこそ、彼女の有罪を証明する 決定的な証拠です。
English: That is also another piece of evidence that incriminates Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: 犯人は、カオを見られずに現場から 逃げるために、着ぐるみを使った。
English: That costume was used to hide the real killer's identity as they fled the crime scene.

Japanese: ケースの中に、着ぐるみがあるのを 知っていた人物は‥‥？
English: Now, who could have known that there was just such a costume inside the guitar case...?

Japanese: 被害者自身と、それを用意した 人物だけ‥‥
English: It could only have been the person who prepared the costume for the victim.

Japanese: その人物とは‥‥ 華宮　霧緒さん、あなたです！
English: And that person is... you, Ms. Adrian Andrews!

Japanese: しかし！　ギターケースに 彼女の指紋はなかった！
English: But Ms. Andrews' fingerprints were nowhere to be found on the guitar case!

Japanese: 証人が手袋をしていなかったのは、 キサマが立証したはずだッ！
English: And it was you who proved that she was not wearing gloves at the time!

Japanese: ‥‥！ た、たしかにそうです！
English: Th-That's right!

Japanese: ‥‥霧緒さんが、ケースに 指紋を残すハズはない。
English: That's because she did not intend on leaving any prints.

Japanese: ケースにさわったことがバレれば、 その理由を聞かれることになる。
English: If anyone had found out that she had touched the case, they would have asked her why.

Japanese: タオルか何かを使って、指紋が 残らないようにしたのでしょう。
English: So to avoid leaving any prints, she used a towel or something else to open it.

Japanese: しかし！ トマトジュースはちがう！
English: But! The glass of tomato juice is a different story!

Japanese: 霧緒さんは、グラスに わざと指紋を残すことで、
English: Ms. Andrews purposefully left her fingerprints on the glass to show that yes, indeed,

Japanese: “動揺した発見者”の フリをしたのです！
English: she was the classic "dazed discoverer" of a dead body!

Japanese: ‥‥きゃああッ！
English: Aaaaaah!

Japanese: そしてさらに、この写真がある！
English: And to top it all off, there is this photo!

Japanese: 現場から立ち去る、犯人の すがたが写っています。
English: A photo of the killer as they exited the scene of the crime.

Japanese: このトノサマンが オートロさんのハズはない！
English: No reasonable person on Earth can believe this Nickel Samurai is Mr. Engarde!

Japanese: ‥‥背が低すぎますからね！
English: He would be much too short for his own costume if it was him.

Japanese: そういえば、霧緒さん。
English: Speaking of how tall people are...

Japanese: あなたも、背は小さな ほうでしたね‥‥？
English: Ms. Andrews, you're also kind of short in stature, are you not?

Japanese: も‥‥もうやめて‥‥
English: P-Please... Stop...

Japanese: さあ！　どうですか、霧緒さん！
English: Well, how about it, Ms. Andrews!?

Japanese: うう‥‥
English: Um...

Japanese: （‥‥今度こそ、キマリだ！）
English: (I've got her this time!)

Japanese: 証人‥‥？
English: Ms. Andrews...?

Japanese: ‥‥証言を、拒否します。 I...
English: I refuse... to testify.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって‥‥？
English: What was that...?

Japanese: こ、この国の法律では‥‥
English: Th-There's a law...

Japanese: 自分の不利になるかもしれない 証言は‥‥拒否できるはずです！
English: It says I can't be forced to testify about something... if it can incriminate me!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに‥‥
English: Well, yes...

Japanese: 証人の言うとおりです。
English: You are absolutely correct, Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: 証言することで、自分を罪に おとしいれてしまう場合のみ‥‥
English: The law does provide us with a way to avoid self- incrimination...

Japanese: 証人は、証言を 拒否するケンリがあります。
English: by allowing a witness to not testify if the testimony can cause damage to themselves.

Japanese: なんですって！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: （“証言の拒否”‥‥ シロートの発想じゃない！）
English: ("Pleading the fifth" is not something most people would think to do on the spot!)

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥きのう、 オートロさんの控え室で‥‥）
English: (Actually... Thinking back to yesterday in Mr. Engarde's room...)

Japanese: ‥‥華宮　霧緒ッ！
English: Adrian Andrews!

Japanese: は、はい‥‥
English: Y-Yes...?

Japanese: さっきの話‥‥ 真剣に考えておくことね！
English: Think hard about what we just discussed. Understood!?

Japanese: え、ええ‥‥
English: A-Alright...

Japanese: （‥‥あのときだ‥‥）
English: (That's it.)

Japanese: （あのとき、狩魔　冥が ‥‥入れ知恵したんだ‥‥）
English: (That's when Franziska planted this idea into her head...)

Japanese: （あぶなくなったら “証言を拒否しろ！”と‥‥）
English: (She must have told Ms. Andrews to not testify if things looked bad.)

Japanese: なるほどくん。 ‥‥あなたの立証はミゴトだった。
English: You did a good job proving everything up to this point, Phoenix.

Japanese: でも‥‥１つだけ、 足りないものがあるの。
English: But there is still one thing you haven't done.

Japanese: “足りないもの”‥‥？
English: Something I haven't done...?

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥
English: Heh heh heh.

Japanese: ‥‥どうした？　弁護人。 もう、手品のタネは切れたのかな？
English: What's wrong, Wright? Are you finished already? Run out of evidence?

Japanese: どういうことですか‥‥御剣検事！
English: What is so humorous, Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: 裁判長なら おわかりだろう‥‥。
English: I'm sure you realize this as well, Your Honor...

Japanese: ただ今の弁護人の立証など ‥‥なんのイミもないことを。
English: But, everything the good lawyer here has proven up to this point is meaningless.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: キサマの立証は‥‥ すべて、状況証拠ばかりだ。
English: Everything you have proven is circumstantial.

Japanese: 状況証拠‥‥？
English: Circumstantial...?

Japanese: 彼女が藤見野　イサオを殺害した という、決定的な証拠‥‥
English: Yes, circumstantial. You have yet to provide a single piece of definitive proof.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなもの、 １つもないではないかッ！
English: Proof that Ms. Andrews, did in fact, harbor a wish to murder Mr. Corrida!

Japanese: しょ、証人！　あなたは ‥‥藤見野　イサオさんを‥‥？
English: M-Ms. Andrews! You... Did you want to kill Mr. Corrida...?

Japanese: 自分を罪におとしいれる可能性を コンキョに、証言を拒否します。
English: I believe this may lead to me incriminating myself, so I will abstain from answering.

Japanese: しかし、証人‥‥
English: But Ms. Andrews...

Japanese: それでは、罪を認めるのと いっしょですよ‥‥？
English: If you do that, it would be the same as admitting your guilt, don't you think...?

Japanese: ‥‥でも、立証されたわけでは ありません。
English: Maybe so, or maybe not. There is nothing to prove it either way.

Japanese: 私の言う“罪”が何を指すのかすら ‥‥あなたがたには、わからない。
English: Besides, you don't even know what crime I would be "guilty" of due to my silence.

Japanese: （くそ‥‥開きなおったか‥‥！）
English: (NO!! She's taking that defiant attitude again!)

Japanese: ち、千尋さん！　どうすれば‥‥
English: M-Mia! What should we do...?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ここへきて、 サイアクの事態ね。
English: Somehow, we've landed in the worst possible situation.

Japanese: ‥‥ここで１つの決断を 下さなければならないようです。
English: I think we have reached a certain conclusion at this point in time.

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒‥‥彼女は証言を 拒否しました。
English: Ms. Adrian Andrews has refused to testify.

Japanese: しかし‥‥検察側の主張どおり、 彼女が殺人を犯したという‥‥
English: And the defense's theory that she is the actual murderer...

Japanese: 直接的な証拠は存在しない。
English: has not been fully substantiated with solid definitive proof.

Japanese: ‥‥なんですって‥‥！
English: But! That's not true!

Japanese: この場合、私にできることは １つしかありません。
English: In this situation, there is only one thing this court can do.

Japanese: それは‥‥審理の延期です。
English: And that is to declare a recess.
R-Recess...!?

Japanese: 検察側・弁護側に、さらなる 調査を要請します。
English: I request that both the prosecution and the defense look further into this matter.

Japanese: ‥‥そして、また明日の審理で‥‥
English: And at tomorrow's trial...
(T-Tomorrow...!?)

Japanese: （ぼくたちに ‥‥“明日”はないんだ！）
English: (We don't have a "tomorrow"!)

Japanese: （今日中に無罪判決を モギ取らなければ‥‥）
English: (If we don't get a not guilty verdict today, then...)

Japanese: 待ってください！ ‥‥それじゃあ‥‥おそすぎる！
English: Please wait, Your Honor! Th...That's not necessary!

Japanese: 審理を‥‥審理を！ おねがいです、つづけてください！
English: The trial... Please continue the trial!

Japanese: 何をあせっている‥‥？
English: What are you sweating for...?

Japanese: キサマの依頼人のイノチが １日、長らえたではないか。
English: Your client is getting one more day to live, isn't he?

Japanese: 問題は‥‥そこじゃない！
English: That... That's not it! This isn't about that.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣ッ！ きみにも、犯人はわかってるはず！
English: Edgeworth! I know you know who the real killer is!

Japanese: たのむ‥‥審理をつづけてくれ！
English: Please... Let the trial continue!

Japanese: 判決を持ちこすことで‥‥ あの子のイノチが‥‥
English: If I don't get the verdict... then Maya...

Japanese: この証人が証言に応じない以上‥‥ 審理の続行は不可能です。
English: But it's impossible to continue as long as the witness refuses to testify.

Japanese: それでは、本日はこれで‥‥
English: Now then, this court is...

Japanese: ‥‥審理をつづけることは 不可能ではない‥‥。
English: It is not impossible for this trial to continue.

Japanese: み‥‥御剣検事！ い、いったい‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth! Wh-What are you...

Japanese: “みずからを罪に落とす証言を 拒否することができる”‥‥
English: It's true Ms. Andrews holds the right against self- incrimination; however,

Japanese: それならば、その“罪”と 関係のない証言は‥‥
English: if the topic of conversation were something unrelated to whatever she may be guilty of,

Japanese: 拒否することはできない！
English: then she has no right to withhold testimony!

Japanese: そ、それはそのとおりですが‥‥
English: Y-Yes, that is very true, but...

Japanese: ‥‥じつは、１つ‥‥ 気になっていることがある。
English: Actually, there is one little thing that I'm curious about.

Japanese: 証人。
English: Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: 死体を発見したとき、証人は グラスにジュースをそそいでいる。
English: When you found the victim's dead body, you poured yourself a glass of juice.

Japanese: そ、それが‥‥何か？
English: Y-Yes... And...?

Japanese: ‥‥私には、 どうも不自然に感じるのだ。
English: I can't help but think how unnatural that is.

Japanese: ふつう、死体を見つけたら、 そんなヨユウなどないはずだ。
English: Usually when one finds a body, they are shaken up, not stirring a glass of juice.

Japanese: 不自然に‥‥？ でも、あれは‥‥
English: So my actions were "unusual"? But I've already...

Japanese: 待ちたまえ。
English: Before you speak, I want to state that if you have a reason behind your actions,

Japanese: 理由があったのならば、 ‥‥証言していただきたい。
English: I would like you to testify to that effect.

Japanese: 証言‥‥！
English: Testify...!?

Japanese: 裁判長！　証人が死体を 発見したときのことを‥‥
English: Your Honor! I would like to request that the witness testify again

Japanese: もう一度、証言させて いただきたい！
English: as to what happened when she first discovered the victim's body!

Japanese: これはいかなるイミにおいても、 罪におとしいれる可能性はない！
English: Whatever we find out in this testimony should in no way implicate the witness.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥今日の御剣検事‥‥ まったく、読めないわ‥‥
English: I don't know what it is about Edgeworth today, but I can't get a good read off of him.

Japanese: 私たちの味方なのか、 それとも敵なのか‥‥
English: Is he friend or foe? I just don't know...

Japanese: 検察側の要請を認めます。 証人は、証言をおねがいします。
English: The court acknowledges the prosecution's request. Ms. Andrews, if you please.

Japanese: ～死体を発見したときのこと～
English: -- When I Found the Body --

Japanese: あのジュース‥‥自分のために 用意したわけじゃないんです。
English: That glass of juice... I didn't really pour it for myself.

Japanese: あの部屋に入ったとき、部屋が 荒らされていてビックリしました。
English: I was surprised when I walked into the room and saw it in that messy state.

Japanese: そして、あの人‥‥藤見野が、 部屋のすみでぐったりしていて。
English: And Juan... He was sitting slumped over and tired-looking in the corner.

Japanese: それを見て、まさか死んでるなんて 思わなかったんです。
English: When I saw him sitting like that, the thought that he was dead didn't cross my mind.

Japanese: てっきり、気絶してると思ったの。 それで‥‥彼のためにジュースを。
English: To be honest, I thought he had just fainted or something. So I went to pour him some juice.

Japanese: 彼が死んでることがわかって‥‥ 花ビンを割ってしまったんです。
English: When I realized that he was dead... That's when I knocked the flower vase over.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥あのグラスは、 被害者のために用意した‥‥と。
English: Hmm... So you poured that glass of juice for the victim.

Japanese: なぜ、さっきは そう証言しなかったのですか？
English: Why didn't you say so in your earlier testimony?

Japanese: ‥‥こんな細かいこと、必要ないと 思ったので‥‥。
English: ...I didn't think I needed to include something so trivial.

Japanese: なるほどくん。これは‥‥ 最後のチャンスよ。
English: Phoenix. Please be careful here.

Japanese: この証言に問題がなければ、 今度こそ‥‥
English: If you can't find anything wrong with this testimony, then there's nothing left...

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I know.

Japanese: （御剣のヤツ‥‥いったい、 何を考えているんだ‥‥？）
English: (Edgeworth... What the heck is going on in that brain of yours...?)

Japanese: では、弁護人。 ‥‥尋問をおねがいします。
English: Now then, Mr. Wright. You may begin your cross- examination.

Japanese: ～死体を発見したときのこと～
English: -- When I Found the Body --

Japanese: あのジュース‥‥自分のために 用意したわけじゃないんです。
English: That glass of juice... I didn't really pour it for myself.

Japanese: あの部屋に入ったとき、部屋が 荒らされていてビックリしました。
English: I was surprised when I walked into the room and saw it in that messy state.

Japanese: そして、あの人‥‥藤見野が、 部屋のすみでぐったりしていて。
English: And Juan... He was sitting slumped over and tired-looking in the corner.

Japanese: それを見て、まさか死んでるなんて 思わなかったんです。
English: When I saw him sitting like that, the thought that he was dead didn't cross my mind.

Japanese: てっきり、気絶してると思ったの。 それで‥‥彼のためにジュースを。
English: To be honest, I thought he had just fainted or something. So I went to pour him some juice.

Japanese: 彼が死んでることがわかって‥‥ 花ビンを割ってしまったんです。
English: When I realized that he was dead... That's when I knocked the flower vase over.

Japanese: あぶないところだったわね。
English: This is what it all comes down to.

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥？
English: Huh?

Japanese: 最後のさいご‥‥ 彼女は、気をゆるしてしまった。
English: This is the absolute end for both sides... And Adrian is letting her guard down.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。トドメよ！
English: Phoenix, now is our best chance yet to kill the prosecution's case!

Japanese: （そんなマズいコト言ってるか？ 霧緒さん‥‥）
English: (Isn't that a bit harsh...? Ms. Andrews... Will you tell us the truth this time...?)

Japanese: でも‥‥あの部屋には、 他にはだれも‥‥
English: But... There wasn't anyone else in the room, right?

Japanese: いませんでした、モチロン。
English: Of course not.

Japanese: ということは‥‥ だれのために？
English: Then... who did you pour it for?

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。モノゴトには、 順序というものがあります。
English: Mr. Wright. There is a rhyme and reason for everything.

Japanese: もうちょっと、お待ち いただけますか‥‥？
English: Can you wait just a little longer for it to be revealed to you?

Japanese: “だまって聞いてろ” ‥‥そういうことだ。
English: In other words, Wright, "Be quiet and listen."

Japanese: そういうことです。
English: Precisely! I couldn't have said it better myself.

Japanese: うう‥‥
English: Unnngh...

Japanese: “荒らされていた”‥‥
English: So, it was a "mess"?

Japanese: あなたと藤見野さんが 争った結果なのでは‥‥？
English: Are you sure it wasn't messy because of your fight with Mr. Corrida...?

Japanese: 証拠がなくアセる気持ちは わからないでもないが‥‥
English: I understand your frustrations at not being able to prove your theory. However...

Japanese: 当てこすりを言う前に、 証拠を探したまえ！
English: Before you go accusing people of crimes, go find yourself some evidence!

Japanese: それで？　証人はそれから、 何を見たのか‥‥？
English: And then, what did you see next, witness?

Japanese: ぐったり‥‥？
English: Slumped over?

Japanese: ええ。イスに座って‥‥ アタマをたれて。
English: Yes, he was just sitting there with his head tilted forward, eyes closed.

Japanese: まるで‥‥ 眠っているみたいでした。
English: He really looked like he was sleeping.

Japanese: （‥‥気のせいか？ この、ミョーな感じは‥‥）
English: (...Is it just me or did that right there sound a little... odd?)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥なんだと 思ったんですか？
English: Then what did cross your mind?

Japanese: グランプリを逃して、 ひとりで暴れたのか、って‥‥。
English: I thought maybe he had smashed everything up in anger because he lost the Grand Prix.

Japanese: それで、つかれて 眠ってしまったのだろう‥‥
English: And then felt tired after his rampage, so he decided to take a nap...

Japanese: そんなふうに思いました。
English: Anyways, that's what I thought...

Japanese: “亡くなっている”とは 思いもよらなかったワケですか。
English: I see. So you didn't think he was dead at all.

Japanese: キゼツ‥‥？
English: You thought he fainted...?

Japanese: 最初は“眠っているんだ” と思ったんですけど。
English: I thought he was asleep at first.

Japanese: 部屋が、あまりにひどく 荒らされていたから‥‥
English: But then, the room was in such a messy state...

Japanese: もしかしたら、だれかと ケンカでもしたのか、と‥‥。
English: I thought maybe he had gotten into a fight with someone...

Japanese: それで、証人はグラスに ジュースを‥‥？
English: And that's when you went to pour the glass of juice?

Japanese: ええ。彼、なかなか目が さめないヒトだったので‥‥
English: Yes. He always has a hard time waking up.

Japanese: いつも、トマトジュースを 飲んでいたんです。
English: So Juan always has a glass of tomato juice to drink.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥なるほど。 それで、どうなりましたか？
English: Hmm... I see. And after that, what happened next?

Japanese: どうして“死んでいる”と わかりましたか？
English: And how did you come to realize that he was, in fact, dead?

Japanese: いくらカラダをゆすっても 返事がなかったので‥‥
English: I shook him over and over, but I never got a response...

Japanese: グラスをドレッサーに置いて、 手首のミャクをとってみたんです。
English: So I set the glass down on the dresser and tried to take his pulse.

Japanese: わ、私‥‥おどろいてしまって ‥‥よろめいて‥‥
English: I... I was shocked... and staggered backward...

Japanese: “花ビンを落とした” ‥‥そういうわけか‥‥
English: And knocked the flower vase over. So that's what happened.

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: ...Yes.

Japanese: “死んでいるとは 思わなかった”‥‥？
English: So you honestly didn't think he was dead when you found him?

Japanese: ええ。だって‥‥
English: No, not at all...

Japanese: この光景を目撃したのに、 ですか‥‥？
English: Even though this is what you saw when you discovered the body?

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか！
English: Wh-What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: 見てのとおりです。
English: Isn't it obvious, Your Honor?

Japanese: イサオさんのムネには、 ナイフが突き立っていたんですよ！
English: There is a knife sticking straight out of Mr. Corrida's chest!

Japanese: だれがどう見たって‥‥ リッパな死体です！
English: Anyone who saw this scene would have immediately thought that here was a dead man!

Japanese: あ‥‥あ、それは‥‥その‥‥
English: Ah... Um... That's... Well, you see...

Japanese: これを見て“気絶している”と カンちがいして、
English: I doubt a single person in the world would mistake this for someone who fainted,

Japanese: ノンキに飲み物を作る人間など、 いるわけがありません！
English: and then so nonchalantly go pour something to drink!

Japanese: つ‥‥つまり‥‥？
English: Y-Your point is...?
