Japanese: 霧緒さん！
English: Ms. Andrews!

Japanese: ‥‥今の証言、 まったくのデタラメです！
English: Your testimony just now... It was all one giant lie!
Ungh!

Japanese: そして、今の決定的な ウソこそが‥‥
English: And your lie has proven one thing very clearly.

Japanese: あなたが犯人であるという、 何よりの証拠なのです！
English: That you are the real killer!

Japanese: いやあああああああああアアアアア アアアアァァァァァァァァァァッ！
English: NOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO OOOOOOOOoooooooooo oooooooooooooooooooo!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥今度こそ、 すべてがハッキリしたようですな。
English: It looks like the defense has somehow brought the ugly truth to light.

Japanese: やはり‥‥王都楼　真悟さんは、 無実だった‥‥
English: The defendant, Mr. Matt Engarde, is not guilty after all...

Japanese: そんな‥‥そんなハズはない！ まちがってる‥‥
English: That... But that's impossible! You're wrong...

Japanese: しょ‥‥証人！
English: M-Ms. Andrews! Try to have some composure!

Japanese: ‥‥私じゃない‥‥ 私はやっていないわ！
English: It... It wasn't me... It wasn't me, I tell you!

Japanese: 王都楼よ！　‥‥アイツが‥‥ アイツが殺したのよ！
English: It was Matt! I swear it! He's the one who killed Juan!

Japanese: しかし、あなたは先ほどから、 証言を拒否している！
English: But you were the one who refused to testify!

Japanese: “自分を罪におとしいれる” という理由で！
English: And your reason for not doing so was that you "might" end up incriminating yourself!

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: Th-That's because...

Japanese: 証人に、最後にもう一度だけ チャンスを与えます。
English: Ms. Andrews, I will give you one last chance.

Japanese: 霧緒さん。あなたの“罪”とは いったい、なんですか‥‥？
English: What exactly are you hiding that may "incriminate" you?

Japanese: しょ‥‥証言を‥‥ I... I...
‥‥証言を、拒否‥‥します。
English: I refuse... to testify.

Japanese: ‥‥これ以上、 審理の必要は認めません。
English: Then there is no need for this court to continue any further.

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟の無実は、 ハッキリ証明されました！
English: Mr. Matt Engarde's innocence has been clearly demonstrated.

Japanese: （終わった‥‥のか‥‥？）
English: (Is... Is it... over...?)

Japanese: （これで‥‥これで、ぼくたちは 真相にたどりついたのか‥‥？）
English: (Have we... Have we found the truth at last...?)

Japanese: ‥‥どうしたの？　なるほどくん。
English: What's wrong, Phoenix?

Japanese: いつもなら‥‥
English: Usually...

Japanese: いつもなら、真犯人はみんな、 自分の罪を認めています。
English: Well, usually, the real killer confesses his or her guilt.

Japanese: 考えてみれば、今回が初めてです ‥‥罪を認めなかった犯人は‥‥
English: And now that I think about it, this is the first time someone hasn't.

Japanese: それでは‥‥王都楼　真悟に、 判決を言いわたしたいと思います。
English: Now then, I would like to hand down my verdict for Mr. Matt Engarde.

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: この時点で 判決を下すことはできない‥‥
English: The prosecution feels that it would be premature to pass down a verdict at this time.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって‥‥
English: Wh-What...?

Japanese: 理由はカンタンだ。
English: The reason is quite simple.

Japanese: この証人は、まだ本当のことを 証言していない。
English: This witness has yet to speak the absolute real truth.

Japanese: い‥‥いったい、それは どういう‥‥
English: The "absolute real truth"...? What are you...?

Japanese: 証人‥‥ まだ、わからないのか。
English: Witness... Don't you understand yet?
...Huh?

Japanese: だれがキサマに 入れ知恵したのかは知らん。
English: I don't know who planted this silly idea in your head,

Japanese: しかし。 キサマが沈黙を守れば‥‥
English: but as long as you "protect" yourself through your silence,

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟は、無罪だ。 そして、そのかわりに‥‥
English: Matt Engarde will go free. And in his place...

Japanese: キサマが真犯人として、 その罪を着ることになるのだぞ！
English: YOU will become the guilty party!

Japanese: ‥‥う‥‥ウソ‥‥ウソよ。 私‥‥私、そんなの信じないわ！
English: ...Th-That's... That's a lie! I... I don't believe you!

Japanese: なんだと‥‥？
English: What...?

Japanese: だ、だって‥‥言われたから。 私がしゃべったら‥‥
English: I... I was told... If I spoke...

Japanese: ゼンブ、おしまいだ、って‥‥ 王都楼を有罪にできない、って‥‥
English: If I spoke, then it would be all over... And Matt would never be declared guilty...

Japanese: （な‥‥何を言いだすんだ！）
English: (Wh-What in the world is she talking about!? Has she lost it!?)

Japanese: ‥‥私‥‥しゃべれない‥‥ こ‥‥怖いから‥‥
English: I... I can't speak about it... I'm too scared...

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事ね。
English: It's Franziska von Karma...
Huh...?

Japanese: おぼえているでしょう？ なるほどくん。
English: Don't you remember, Phoenix?

Japanese: あの証人は、つねにだれかの 言うことを盲信して生きている。
English: Ms. Andrews lives by gripping tightly onto the words of another.

Japanese: 自分自身というものを、 持つことができないから。
English: Because she doesn't have the strength to believe in herself.

Japanese: じゃ‥‥じゃあ、彼女は、今‥‥
English: Th...Then, right now... Ms. Andrews is...

Japanese: きのう、あの証人は狩魔検事から 絶対的な命令を受けたの。
English: Yesterday, she was tossed a life-saver by Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: “何があっても証言するな。 そうすれば王都楼は有罪になる”
English: "Don't say a word, no matter what happens. If you do, Matt Engarde will be acquitted."

Japanese: 彼女は今‥‥それを信じている。 ココロの底から。それだけを‥‥
English: Ms. Andrews undyingly believes in those words right now, and is clinging onto them...

Japanese: （ど‥‥どうすればいい‥‥！）
English: (Th-Then what should we do!?)

Japanese: （‥‥こんな状況‥‥ はじめてだぞ！）
English: (This... This is the first time I've ever come across anything like this!)

Japanese: （彼女‥‥華宮　霧緒が‥‥ 真犯人のはずだろ！）
English: (But Ms. Andrews has to be the killer, right!?)

Japanese: （あとは、判決を‥‥無罪判決を 待つだけ‥‥それだけのはず！）
English: (All we have to do now is... is get our not guilty... That is my only priority!)

Japanese: ‥‥私じゃない！　私は 殺していないわ！
English: It wasn't me! I'm begging you, please believe me! I didn't kill Juan!

Japanese: ‥‥助けて‥‥おねがい‥‥ だれか‥‥！
English: Help... Please... Someone... Help me...!

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.
Y-Yes, Your Honor!

Japanese: このままでは、収拾がつきません。 あなたの考えを聞きましょう。
English: The court can't continue on like this, therefore I'd like to hear what you intend to do.

Japanese: （ぼくの考え‥‥！ ど‥‥どうすればいいんだ！）
English: (What I intend to do...!? Wh-What am I supposed to do!? What am I supposed to say!?)

Japanese: 成歩堂！　もう一度、 よく考えるんだ！
English: Wright! I suggest you think very carefully about this!

Japanese: この証人が何をしたか！ そして、何をしなかったのか‥‥！
English: Think about what this witness did, and what she did NOT do!

Japanese: この事件の真犯人は、だれなのか！
English: And think about who is the real mastermind behind this crime!

Japanese: （真犯人は‥‥決まってる！）
English: (Who's the real mastermind? Isn't that obvious!?)

Japanese: （そこで泣いてる女しかいない！ ‥‥そうだろう‥‥？）
English: (There's no one else it could be except the woman crying over there! ...Right?)

Japanese: さあ！　キサマの結論は‥‥ どうなんだ！　成歩堂　龍一！
English: Come now! What will you do!? What kind of man are you, Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: （なんと言われようと‥‥ 犯人は、霧緒さんしかいない！）
English: (No matter what she says... Ms. Andrews is the only person it could be!)

Japanese: （それに‥‥ぼくは‥‥）
English: (Not to mention...)

Japanese: （今日中にかならず、無罪判決を 勝ちとらなきゃならないんだ！）
English: (I don't have a choice. I have to win a complete acquittal today!)

Japanese: さ‥‥裁判長！
English: Y-Your Honor!

Japanese: 証拠は‥‥これで じゅうぶんです！
English: The defense believes there is enough evidence to substantiate our claim!

Japanese: 今すぐ、無罪判決を要求します！
English: Therefore, we motion that this court passes a verdict of "not guilty" as soon as possible!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 見そこなったぞ‥‥成歩堂！
English: I'm disappointed in you, Wright!

Japanese: この１年間‥‥キサマは 何を見てきたのだッ！
English: What have you learned in this past year!?

Japanese: （今日中に無罪判決を勝ちとる。 ぼくには、それしかない‥‥！）
English: (I have to win a complete acquittal today... There's no way around that!)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥）
（ナットクできない！ 霧緒さんの、あの表情‥‥）
English: (I can't bring myself to do it like this! Not when she's making a face like that...)

Japanese: ‥‥証人！
English: Ms. Andrews!

Japanese: いったい‥‥あなたは、 何をかくしているんですかッ！
English: I would like to know what you are really hiding!

Japanese: べ‥‥弁護人！ あなたは‥‥何を！
English: M-Mr. Wright! Are you sure you know what you're doing!?

Japanese: オートロさんは無罪判決を受ける！ なぜなら、あなたが真犯人だから！
English: Sure, Mr. Engarde would get an acquittal, but in his place, you would be found guilty!

Japanese: それで‥‥この法廷は 終わろうとしているんですよ！
English: Is this... Is this how you really want this trial to end!?

Japanese: ‥‥ウソ‥‥よ！　そんな‥‥ 私をだますつもりね！
English: B-Be quiet... How dare you!? You... You're trying to trick me!

Japanese: もう、よろしい！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 御剣検事。 あなたは、よくやったと思います。
English: I commend you for trying, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: しかし‥‥、弁護人が言うとおり 真実はアキラカです。
English: However, it's clear that the defense's theory is the truth.

Japanese: ‥‥ち‥‥ちがうわ‥‥
English: ...Y...You're wrong...

Japanese: やむをえないな。
English: Such a shame...

Japanese: これだけは やりたくなかったのだが‥‥
English: I had hoped things wouldn't come to this, however...

Japanese: な、なんですか‥‥御剣検事。
English: Wh-What is it, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 証人。キサマがどうしても 証言しないと言うのならば‥‥
English: Ms. Andrews. Since you absolutely refuse to testify...

Japanese: その理由を、この法廷中に 暴露するしかないな。
English: It falls on my shoulders to disclose this to the court.

Japanese: ‥‥や、やめて‥‥
ど、どういうことですか！ 御剣検事！
English: M-Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: ‥‥そこの証人は‥‥ ある種、病気と言っていい。
English: This witness... How should I put this... She has an illness.

Japanese: な‥‥ッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: 彼女は、その病気ゆえに、 自殺をくわだてたこともある。
English: And because of this illness, she has tried to commit suicide in the past.

Japanese: や‥‥やめてください‥‥！
English: S-Stop... Please stop...!

Japanese: かくそうとしても、ムダだ。 ここに、その証拠がある。
English: No matter how much you want to hide it, it's no use. I have the evidence right here.

Japanese: あっ！　そ、それは‥‥！
English: Ah! Th-That's...!

Japanese: （あれは‥‥ ２通の自殺報告書‥‥！）
English: (That's the second part of the suicide report; the attempted suicide report!)

Japanese: さあ‥‥どうする、証人。
English: What will you do now, witness?

Japanese: これから、華宮　霧緒という なさけない人間の本性を‥‥
English: You know what I am about to do, don't you?
I will now reveal to the court,

Japanese: 私はすべて、ブチまける！
English: the true nature of the pitiful woman known as Adrian Andrews!

Japanese: （“依存”というヒミツ‥‥）
English: (The secret of her co-dependant nature...)

Japanese: （彼女は、それを知られるのを 何より怖れているんだ‥‥）
English: (Having other people know about it scares her more than anything else in the world...)

Japanese: ‥‥おねがい、やめてッ！ そんなことをしたら‥‥
English: Please! Please STOP!! I beg you!! If people find out...

Japanese: そんなことをされたら、 私‥‥私は‥‥
English: If people find out... I... I'll...

Japanese: 証人が“死”を選ぼうと、 私の知ったことではない‥‥。
English: If you're going to say you would "choose death", that is of no concern to me.

Japanese: （御剣‥‥なんてことを‥‥！）
English: (Edgeworth... How can you be so cold...!?)

Japanese: しかし！　キサマが死ぬ前に‥‥
English: However, before you die...

Japanese: 私はかならず、キサマの口から 真実を引きずり出す！
English: I will pull the truth from your breathing lips!

Japanese: ‥‥どんな手段を使っても。
English: No matter what I have to do.

Japanese: さあ、どうするのだッ！ ‥‥私は、どっちでもかまわない。
English: So, will you tell the court yourself, or shall I!? Either is fine with me.

Japanese: 話します。だから‥‥ 助けてください‥‥
English: I'll talk. But please... Help me...

Japanese: ‥‥もう‥‥何もわからない‥‥。
English: N-Nothing matters anymore...

Japanese: ～霧緒の“罪”とは～
English: -- My "Crime" --

Japanese: 彼を見たとき‥‥キゼツしていると 思った。‥‥ホントです。
English: When I first saw him... I really thought he had fainted. Honest.

Japanese: 彼が死んでいるとわかって‥‥ ふと、計画を思いついたんです。
English: When I realized he was dead... That was when I formulated my plan.

Japanese: ろうかに人影がないのをたしかめて いったん楽屋にもどったわ。
English: Once I made sure there was no one in the hallway, I made a dash back to Matt's room.

Japanese: そして‥‥ナイフを死体に刺して、 ボタンをむしり取ったの。
English: And then... I stabbed Juan's dead body with the knife, and ripped off the button.

Japanese: すべてが終わって、帰ろうとしたら ‥‥困ったことになった。
English: Just when I finished and was returning to Matt's room... I had a bit of an inconvenience.

Japanese: それで‥‥トノサマンの着ぐるみを 使わせてもらったの。
English: And that's why... That's why I ended up using the Nickel Samurai costume.

Japanese: し‥‥死体に、ナイフを！ いったい、なぜ！
English: S-Stabbed the body!? With the knife!? But why would you do that!?

Japanese: 決まってるでしょう？ ‥‥罪を着せるためです。
English: Isn't it obvious? To pin the blame on a certain person...

Japanese: ‥‥そこの、ヒキョウな男に‥‥！
English: A certain cowardly man!
Wh...

Japanese: なんということでしょう‥‥
English: What do you mean by all of this...?

Japanese: 少し、手間どってしまったが‥‥
English: It might take this court a little while to understand, but...

Japanese: これこそが‥‥真実だ。
English: This is the truth.

Japanese: 真犯人は、王都楼よ！ キタナイ男‥‥ユルせない！
English: The real killer is Matt! That scumbag of a man! I'll never forgive him!

Japanese: 今度こそ‥‥ 罪をつぐなわせてやるの！
English: He's trying to escape his guilt again! Just like last time!
("Last time"...?)

Japanese: ‥‥彼女は、藤見野　イサオの ムネに、ナイフを刺した。
English: So, Ms. Andrews stabbed the victim, Juan Corrida, in the chest with the knife.

Japanese: しかしそれは‥‥殺害する ためではなかった。
English: However, she didn't do it with murder in mind.

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟の罪を暴くため‥‥
English: She did it with the intent of framing Matt Engarde for the murder...

Japanese: それこそが！ 彼女の“罪”だったのだ！
English: And this! This is her "crime"!

Japanese: （ま‥‥まさか！ そんなはずはない‥‥）
English: (Wh-WHAT!? How is this possible!?)

Japanese: （華宮　霧緒こそが‥‥ 犯人のハズじゃなかったのか！）
English: (I mean... Wasn't Ms. Andrews supposed to be the real murderer!?)

Japanese: ‥‥では、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Wright. Please get over your shock and commence the cross-examination.

Japanese: ～霧緒の“罪”とは～
English: -- My "Crime" --

Japanese: 彼を見たとき‥‥キゼツしていると 思った。‥‥ホントです。
English: When I first saw him... I really thought he had fainted. Honest.

Japanese: 彼が死んでいるとわかって‥‥ ふと、計画を思いついたんです。
English: When I realized he was dead... That was when I formulated my plan.

Japanese: ろうかに人影がないのをたしかめて いったん楽屋にもどったわ。
English: Once I made sure there was no one in the hallway, I made a dash back to Matt's room.

Japanese: そして‥‥ナイフを死体に刺して、 ボタンをむしり取ったの。
English: And then... I stabbed Juan's dead body with the knife, and ripped off the button.

Japanese: すべてが終わって、帰ろうとしたら ‥‥困ったことになった。
English: Just when I finished and was returning to Matt's room... I had a bit of an inconvenience.

Japanese: それで‥‥トノサマンの着ぐるみを 使わせてもらったの。
English: And that's why... That's why I ended up using the Nickel Samurai costume.

Japanese: （認めたくないけど、この証言‥‥ “真実”のニオイがする）
English: (I don't want to concede, but this testimony... it rings of the truth...)

Japanese: ごめんなさい、なるほどくん‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Phoenix...

Japanese: お、おねがいですから、 あやまらないでください。
English: N-No, please, don't apologize to me. We were both taken by surprise.

Japanese: ごめんなさい‥‥
English: Still, I'm sorry...

Japanese: でも、部屋は荒らされて、 アキラカに格闘のあとだった！
English: But you could tell from the state the room was in, that there must have been a fight!

Japanese: 亡くなっているのがわからなかった ‥‥ホントですか‥‥？
English: Are you telling the truth when you say that you did not know he was dead?

Japanese: 彼‥‥マフラーでクビを しめられたんですよね‥‥。
English: He... had a scarf tied around his neck...

Japanese: マフラーは忍者ナンジャの 衣装でした。だから‥‥
English: But that scarf is a part of the Jammin' Ninja's costume... So...

Japanese: おかしいと思わなかったんです。 それに‥‥、
English: So I didn't think anything about it was strange...

Japanese: カオも‥‥うつむいていて、 よく見えなかった‥‥。
English: His head was also... tilted down a bit, so I couldn't see his face that well...

Japanese: だから私‥‥起こそうと 思って、ジュースを‥‥
English: That's why I thought I'd wake him up... and went to pour the juice...

Japanese: “計画”というのは‥‥？
English: What is this "plan" you had?

Japanese: ハンニンは王都楼だと、 すぐにわかりました。
English: I knew right away the murderer was Matt.

Japanese: 藤見野は‥‥彼の決定的な 弱みを発表しようとしていたから。
English: I knew because Juan... He was going to expose Matt's weakest weakpoint to the world.

Japanese: だから、先手を打って、 口をふさいだんだ‥‥って。
English: So Matt did this to stop Juan, and silence him for good.

Japanese: “王都楼の罪を立証するような 証拠を作り出そう”
English: That's when I thought,

Japanese: ‥‥そう思ったんです。
English: "I should forge some evidence and pin this crime on Matt."

Japanese: それが‥‥あのボタンや、 ナイフだったワケか‥‥
English: So the forged pieces of evidence were the knife and the button...

Japanese: まず、アタマに浮かんだのが ナイフでした。
English: The first thing that came to mind was to plant the knife.

Japanese: それは‥‥ナイフを 取りに行くために？
English: That was so you could get the knife, correct?

Japanese: 食事で使ったナイフには、 王都楼の指紋がついていました。
English: The knife Matt used at dinner had his fingerprints all over it.

Japanese: あれを使えば‥‥警察はかならず、 彼に目をつけるはずだ、って。
English: I thought if I used that, then the police would certainly turn their eyes towards him.

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼は、着ぐるみのまま 寝ていました。
English: Matt was napping with his costume on at the time.

Japanese: 私は、そっとナイフを取って‥‥ 現場にもどったんです。
English: I slipped in, took the knife, and returned to the scene of the crime.

Japanese: あのナイフは‥‥あなたが 刺したんですか。
English: So you were the one to stab the victim with that knife.

Japanese: 今、考えると‥‥ぞっとします。 なんて、ヒドいことを、って。
English: It gives me goosebumps to think about it now... What a horrible thing I did...

Japanese: でも‥‥あのときは。 からだが、勝手に動いたんです。
English: But... At the time, I couldn't control my own body. It moved on its own.

Japanese: ナイフを刺したときに ‥‥ふと、気がつきました。
English: Then, when I stabbed Juan's dead body... I suddenly realized something.

Japanese: ボタンを使えば、王都楼に もっと疑いをかけることができる。
English: If I used the button somehow, I could make Matt look even more suspect.

Japanese: ボタンをむしり取って‥‥被告人の ハカマに挟んだ、というわけか。
English: So you thought to rip one of the buttons off and then plant it in Mr. Engarde's hakama.

Japanese: ええ。そうするつもりでした。
English: Yes... That's what I had planned to do.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、そうカンタンには いかなかったんです。
English: ...But things never go that smoothly, do they?

Japanese: “困ったこと”‥‥？
English: An "inconvenience"...?

Japanese: ろうかには、カメラをかまえた 女性がウロウロしていたんです。
English: There was a woman with a camera at the ready, loitering in the hallway.

Japanese: （ナツミさんだな‥‥）
English: (I'm willing to bet my spikes it was Lotta...)

Japanese: 光線銃をかまえた 女性もウロウロしていて‥‥
English: There was also a woman with a ray gun at the ready pacing back and forth...

Japanese: （オバチャンか‥‥）
English: (That's Ms. Oldbag for you...)

Japanese: 私、週刊誌にスクープ されていましたから‥‥
English: I had already been caught and made into a big scoop for a certain weekly tabloid once,

Japanese: そのまま出ていくことは できなかったんです。
English: so I couldn't very well go out looking like myself and get caught again.

Japanese: その着ぐるみは、 あなたが用意したんですね？
English: You were the one who prepared that costume, weren't you?

Japanese: ええ。英都撮影所から 持ち出して‥‥。
English: Yes. I took it from Global Studios...

Japanese: 授賞式の前日、彼のギターケースに しまったんです。
English: I also put it into Juan's guitar case the day before the award ceremony.

Japanese: “記者会見”のために‥‥？
English: You did this in preparation for the press conference, correct?

Japanese: 藤見野は、その着ぐるみを使って 記者会見をするつもりでした。
English: Yes, Juan wanted to wear that costume and hold a press conference in it.

Japanese: そして‥‥王都楼のヒミツを “暴露”するつもりだったんです。
English: He was going to disclose Matt's big secret there.

Japanese: “ヒミツ”というのは‥‥？
English: And what is this "secret"...?

Japanese: ‥‥私は知りません。
English: ...That, I don't know.

Japanese: とにかく、私が“トノサマン”の すがたで控え室を出れば‥‥
English: Anyway, I thought that if I were to leave Juan's room in the Nickel Samurai costume...

Japanese: “ハンニンは王都楼”と印象づける ことができる‥‥そう思った。
English: then people would think that Matt was the "real" murderer.

Japanese: ギターケースを開けるとき、 指紋には注意しました。
English: I was very careful not to leave any fingerprints when I opened the guitar case.

Japanese: 着ぐるみのことは、ゼッタイ 気づかれたくなかったから‥‥
English: I absolutely did not want anyone to know about the costume.

Japanese: もう、けっこうです！
English: I think we've heard enough!

Japanese: あなたは控え室に帰り、死体から むしり取ってきたボタンを‥‥
English: So, after that, you went back to Mr. Engarde's room and planted the button?

Japanese: 王都楼のハカマに挟みました。
English: ...Into Matt's hakama? Yes.

Japanese: そのあと、着ぐるみをたたんで バッグにしまって‥‥
English: After that, I folded up the costume I was wearing and put it into a bag.

Japanese: ホテルを抜け出して ‥‥始末したんです。
English: Then I snuck it out of the hotel and got rid of it.

Japanese: な‥‥ なんということでしょう‥‥。
English: M-My word... What does all this mean...?

Japanese: ‥‥ミツルギ‥‥と 言いましたね、検事さん。
English: ...Mr. Edgeworth, is it?

Japanese: ハンニンは‥‥あの男‥‥ 王都楼です！
English: The real criminal... is Matt Engarde!!

Japanese: きのう、私‥‥ 女の検事さんに言われたんです。
English: Yesterday... That woman prosecutor sat me down for a talk...

Japanese: （‥‥狩魔　冥か‥‥）
English: (Franziska, huh...)

Japanese: 私がしたコト‥‥ゼッタイに しゃべっちゃダメだ、って。
English: She said that I should under no circumstances confess to what I had done.

Japanese: そうしたら、かならず‥‥ 王都楼を有罪にしてやる、って‥‥
English: That if I just kept quiet, then Matt would be found guilty for sure...

Japanese: 私‥‥それを信じるしか なかったんです‥‥
English: I... I had no choice but to believe in her words...

Japanese: ‥‥この証人がしたことは、 あきらかに法律に反しています。
English: What this witness has done is clearly unlawful.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし‥‥
English: However...

Japanese: その証言をくずすことが できない以上‥‥、
English: As long as her testimony stands,

Japanese: この証人が犯人であるとは 断定できません！
English: we can be certain she is not the real killer!

Japanese: ま、待ってください！
English: W-Wait, Your Honor!

Japanese: 弁護側は、まだ‥‥
English: The defense still...

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。
English: Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥ムリだ。
English: It's pointless.

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒を告発するのは、 現時点では不可能だ。
English: At this point in time, it is not possible to indict Ms. Andrews on anything.

Japanese: そのとおりです。
English: Yes, exactly.

Japanese: 殺人を立証する証拠‥‥。 １つもない、と言わざるを得ない。
English: There isn't a single piece of evidence that points to her as the murderer.

Japanese: 審理は終了します。
English: The cross-examination of this witness is over. And so is today's trial.

Japanese: 証人の犯行は立証できなかった。 ‥‥あきらめなさい、弁護人！
English: You couldn't establish that the witness was the culprit. Please let it go, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥ッ！
English: B-But!

Japanese: 御剣検事。華宮　霧緒の身柄を 拘束しておくように。
English: Mr. Edgeworth, please place Ms. Andrews under arrest for further questioning.

Japanese: ‥‥心得た。 さっそく、留置の手配を‥‥。
English: Understood, Your Honor. The prosecution will arrange for her detention immediately.

Japanese: それでは、 本日は、これにて閉廷！
English: That's all. Court is adjourned for today!

Japanese: （‥‥審理が‥‥終了した‥‥！）
English: (Today's... Today's trial... It's over...!)

Japanese: （無罪判決を‥‥ 勝ちとることができないまま！）
English: (And I didn't win an acquittal!)

Japanese: 証人‥‥。 ちょっとよろしいだろうか。
English: Witness... Would you mind if I asked you something?
(Edgeworth...?)

Japanese: なんでしょうか‥‥？
English: What is it...?

Japanese: 退廷する前に、１つだけ うかがいたい。
English: Before you leave court today, I wondered if I might look at one thing.

Japanese: さっきから気になっていたのだが ‥‥その手に持っている、カード。
English: The card in your hand. It's had my interest for quite some time now.

Japanese: それはいったい‥‥？
English: What exactly is it...?

Japanese: ああ。これですか‥‥？ 弁護士さんにも聞かれましたね。
English: Oh, this...? Mr. Wright also asked about this.

Japanese: 私にもおぼえが なかったんですけど‥‥
English: Although I didn't remember at the time you asked me about it, Mr. Wright,

Japanese: ‥‥ついさっき、思い出しました。 あの日、控え室でひろったんです。
English: I remembered just now. I found this in the room on that day.

Japanese: あの日‥‥？
English: "The room"...?

Japanese: 控え室‥‥？
English: "That day"...?

Japanese: ええ。
English: Yes.

Japanese: 藤見野の死体を発見したときに。
English: I found this card when I discovered Juan's body.

Japanese: ‥‥死体のすぐそばに 置いてあったんです。
English: It was lying there right next to him...

Japanese: 死体のそばに‥‥そのカードが？
English: You found that card... next to the victim's body?

Japanese: つい、ムイシキのうちに‥‥ ポケットにしまったみたいです。
English: I suppose I must have unconsciously slipped it into my pocket...

Japanese: ‥‥でも、関係ないでしょう？ こんなカード。
English: ...But it's not as if this card has any relevance to Juan's murder, right?

Japanese: （まあ、そうだな。 ‥‥ヘンなカードだけど‥‥）
English: (Yeah, I guess not. But it's still a strange card, if you ask me...)

Japanese: （事件の手がかりには‥‥）
English: (But as far as a clue to this case...? I don't see why...)

Japanese: 証人ッ！　そのカード‥‥ こちらにわたすのだッ！　早く！
English: Witness! That card... Give it to me! Hurry!
E-Edgeworth...?

Japanese: キサマは‥‥なんという、 バカなことをしてくれたのだ！
English: Do you have any idea what you have stupidly, yet inadvertently done!?

Japanese: コイツを‥‥今まで 私の目からかくしておくなんて！
English: This... I can't believe you hid this from me all this time!

Japanese: そ、そんなつもりは‥‥私‥‥。
English: I... I didn't mean to...

Japanese: ど‥‥どうしたのかしら‥‥？
English: Wh-What is this all about...?

Japanese: （あんなに感情的な御剣、 見たことがないぞ‥‥）
English: (I've never seen such an emotional Edgeworth in my entire life...)

Japanese: （あのカード‥‥ いったい、なんなんだ‥‥？）
English: (That card... What in the world is it? And what does it mean...!?)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、王都楼　真悟に この場で、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Matt Engarde...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
